Kayıt İşlemleri Hakkında Sıkça Sorulan Sorular
1) Üniversitenize kayıt hakkı kazandım, hangi tarihlerde kayıt yaptırabilirim?
2025 Yılı Yükseköğretim Kurumları Sınavı (YKS) sonuçlarına göre bir programa kayıt hakkı kazanan adayların kayıt işlemleri 10-17 Ekim 2025 tarihleri arasında yapılacaktır. Üniversitemiz ve kayıt süreçleri hakkında bilgi edinmek için
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr
adresini ziyaret edebilirsiniz.
2) Üniversitenize kayıt hakkı kazandım, kayıt işlemleri için izlemem gereken yol ya da yapmam gereken işlemler nelerdir.
İki şekilde kayıt işlemini gerçekleştirebilirsiniz. Birincisi YKS sonuç belgenizde ilan edilen 10-17 Ekim 2025 tarihleri arasında, ilan edilen evrakları temin edip üniversitemize gelerek, ikincisi ise 10-12 Ekim 2025 tarihleri arasında online kayıt yöntemi ile kayıt yaptırabilirsiniz. Yalnız her iki şekilde de kesin kayıt yaptırmadan önce
https://obs.acibadem.edu.tr/oibs/ogrsis/ogr_on_kayit_op.aspx
adresinden giriş yaparak ön kayıt işlemini gerçekleştirmeniz gerekmektedir.
3) Üniversiteye gelmeden online kayıt nasıl yaptırabilirim.
Öncelikle
https://obs.acibadem.edu.tr/oibs/ogrsis/ogr_on_kayit_op.aspx
adresinden giriş yaparak ön kayıt işlemini gerçekleştirmeniz gerekmektedir. Daha sonra https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/ adresinden giriş yaparak “Kayıt İşlemleri ve Kayıt Tarihleri” başlığı altındaki alanı inceleyiniz bu alan sizi adım adım yönlendirecektir.
4) Online kayıt için E-devlet şifremi nereden alabilirim?
E-devlet şifrenizi, üzerinde T.C. kimlik numaranız bulunduğu fotoğraflı bir kimlik (nüfus cüzdanı, pasaport veya ehliyet) ile ibraz ederek yurt içinde PTT Merkez müdürlüklerinden veya yetkili şubelerinden alabilirsiniz.
5) Online kayıt zorunlu mudur, kayıt işlemimi üniversiteye gelerek yaptırabilir miyim?
Elektronik kayıt öğrenci için zorunlu olmayıp isteğe bağlıdır.
6) Online kayıt ile kayıt yaptım, tekrar üniversiteye gelerek işlem yapmam ya da evrak teslim etmem gerekiyor mu?
E-devlet üzerinden kesin kayıt işleminizi tamamladıysanız üniversitemize kayıt işleminizin yapıldığı anlamına gelir tekrar kayıt için gelmenize ve herhangi belge getirmenize gerek yoktur.
7) Üniversitenize yerleştim fakat aynı zamanda bir diğer üniversitede ön lisans\lisans programlarından birinde kaydım bulunmakta, aynı anda her iki üniversitede de okuyabilir miyim?
Aynı anda örgün iki ön lisans veya iki lisans programında kayıtlı olamazsınız. Bu sebeple ne üniversitemize gelerek ne de online kayıt kayıt işleminizi tamamlayamazsınız. Bu durumdaki öğrencilerin öncelikle kayıt tarihleri aralığında kayıtlı oldukları önceki yüksek öğretim kurumundan ilişik kesme işlemini yaptırıp, yöksis’deki öğrencilik durumlarının güncellemesi gerekmektedir.
8) Üniversitenize yerleştim fakat liseden bütünleme\tek dersten dolayı mezun olamadım kayıt hakkımı kullanabilecek miyim?
Kayıt hakkı kazanan ancak ortaöğretim kurumlarından mezun olamayarak bütünleme veya tek ders sınava girecek adayların kayıtlı olduğu lise müdürlüğünden güncel tarihli ve onaylı muhtemel mezuniyet yazısı ile birlikte 10-17 Ekim 2025 tarihleri arasında üniversitemize gelerek geçici kayıtlarını yaptırabilirler. Bu adayların mezun olduklarına ilişkin belgelerini, 31 Aralık 2025 tarihi mesai bitimine kadar yükseköğretim kurumuna ibraz etmeleri halinde Açık Öğretim Liseleri öğrencilerinden bir yükseköğretim kurumuna yerleşen adayların ise mezun olduklarına ilişkin belgelerini 30 Ocak 2026 tarihi mesai bitimine kadar yükseköğretim kurumuna ibraz etmeleri halinde, asıl kayıtları yapılacaktır. Bu tarihe kadar mezun olduklarını belgelemeyenlerin geçici kayıtları silinecek ve bu durumda olup belirlenen tarihlerde geçici kayıtlarını yaptırmayan adayların kayıtları yapılmayacaktır.
9) Üniversitenize belirtilen tarih aralıklarında kayıt yaptıramazsam daha sonra yaptırabilir miyim?
ÖSYM tarafından belirlenen 10-17 Ekim 2025 tarihleri arasında kayıt yaptırmanız gerekmektedir, bu süre içerisinde başvurmayan veya kayıt işlemlerini tamamlamayan adaylar kayıt haklarını kaybedeceklerdir.
11) Kayıt esnasında gelemezsem benim yerime yakınım kayıt işlemlerini tamamlayabilir mi?
Kayıt hakkı kazanan adayların kayıt için bizzat başvurmaları (elektronik kayıt hariç) gerekmektedir. Adayın kendisi olmadan kayıt yapılmayacağı gibi posta ile de kayıt yapılmayacaktır. Ancak noterden vekaletname verdiğiniz bir yakınınız sizin adınıza kayıt işlemini yapabilir yalnız kimlik, öğrenci belgesi vb. belgeler ancak siz üniversitemize geldiğinizde alabilirsiniz
12) Daha önce bir başka yükseköğretim kurumunda almış olduğum dersler var muafiyet işlemi yapılır mı?
Daha önce bir başka yükseköğretim kurumunda almış ve başarmış olduğunuz dersleriniz varsa, kayıt olduktan en geç bir hafta içerisinde kayıt yaptırdığınız fakülte/meslek yüksekokulları sekreterine muafiyet dilekçesi ve ders içeriklerinizle birlikte başvurabilirsiniz.
13) Bu sene eğitime devam edemeyeceğim, kayıt dondurabilir miyim?
Kayıt dondurabilmeniz için öncelikle ilan edilen kayıt tarihleri aralığında kesin kayıt işleminizi yapmanız gerekmektedir. Kesin kayıt yaptırmayan öğrenci kayıtlı öğrenci olamaz dolayısıyla kayıt donduramaz. Kayıt işleminiz bittikten sonra bir dilekçe ile faküle/meslek yüksekokulunuz sekreterliğine başvuru yapabilirsiniz. Kayıt dondurulan süreler içerisinde ödeme ile ilgili bilgileri Mali İşler Müdürlüğümüzden alabilirsiniz.
14) Üniversiteye kayıt olduktan sonra askerlik tecil işlemleri üniversite tarafından yapılacak mı?
Askerlik yaş sınırı sorunu olmayan adayların kayıt işlemleri yapıldıktan sonra tecil işlemleri yapılmaktadır, yalnız aşağıdaki bentlerden birine uyan adaylar için uygulanmak üzere;
a)
7179 sayılı Askeralma Kanununun hükümlerine ve bu konuda Millî Savunma Bakanlığınca yayınlanan prensip emirlerine göre askerlik hizmetlerini erteletme hakkını kaybedenler,
b)
İki veya üç yıllık bir yükseköğretim programını bitirmiş olup dört yıldan daha az süreli bir başka yükseköğretim programına girmek isteyenlerden askerliğini yapmamış olanlar (İki veya üç yıl süreli bir yükseköğretim programından mezun olup askerliğini yapmamış olan adaylar, dört yıl veya daha fazla süreli bir yükseköğretim programına girmek için 2025-YKS'ye başvurabilirler.),
c)
Dört yıl veya daha fazla süreli bir yükseköğretim programını bitirmiş olanlardan askerliğini yapmamış olan adaylar, 2025-YKS sonucunda herhangi bir yükseköğretim programına yerleşmeleri durumunda bu adayların kayıtları yapılacaktır. Ancak bu durumdaki adayların askerlikleri tecil edilmiş ise askerlik tecil sürelerinin bitimine kadar kayıt hakkı kazandıkları programda eğitime devam edebilecek, tecil sürelerinin sona ermesi hâlinde askerlik erteleme haklarını kaybettiklerinden bu durumdaki adayların kayıtları dondurularak, askerlik hizmetlerini yaptıktan sonra eğitime devam edebileceklerdir.
***2025-YKS sonucunda herhangi bir yükseköğretim programına yerleşmeleri durumunda bu adayların kayıtları yapılacaktır. Ancak bu durumdaki adayların askerlikleri tecil edilmiş ise askerlik tecil sürelerinin bitimine kadar kayıt hakkı kazandıkları programda eğitime devam edebilecek, tecil sürelerinin sona ermesi hâlinde askerlik erteleme haklarını kaybettiklerinden bu durumdaki adayların kayıtları dondurularak, askerlik hizmetlerini yaptıktan sonra eğitime devam edebileceklerdir.

Vision
To become one of the world’s distinguished and leading universities that is pioneering, innovative, and exemplary in education, research, and community service.
Mission
To educate rational, competent individuals who draw their strength from scientific knowledge; who are culturally and socially equipped; sensitive to society and the environment; committed to ethical values and Atatürk’s principles; and who possess lifelong learning habits.
To provide contemporary, innovative, and pioneering education and research environments.
To contribute to a sustainable world.
To work toward achieving excellence in education, research, and community contribution by prioritizing scientific thinking.
Core Values
Commitment to scientific knowledge
Academic freedom
Freedom of thought and expression
Fairness
Commitment to ethical values
Respect for diversity
Innovation
Pursuit of excellence
Inclusiveness
Transparency
Professionalism
Rationality
Social accountability
Sustainability

Chairman of Board of Trustees
Mehmet Ali Aydınlar
Chairman of Board of Trustees
Born in 1956 in the Arapgir district of Malatya, Mehmet Ali Aydınlar graduated from Galatasaray Business School in 1977. He began his professional career as a Certified Public Accountant in 1981 and entered the healthcare sector in 1991 with the establishment of Acıbadem Hospital.
Today, he serves as the Chairman of the Board of Directors of Acıbadem Healthcare Group, which operates 18 hospitals and 13 medical centers, along with several support companies in the healthcare sector. Since 2012, he has also been a Board Member of Integrated Healthcare Holdings Berhad and Parkway Pantai Limited. In addition, he continues to serve as the Chairman of the Board of Trustees of Acıbadem University, a foundation university.
Mehmet Ali Aydınlar has received numerous awards for his outstanding achievements. In 1998, he was honored with the Excellence in Management Award at the World Young Entrepreneur Businessman Competition. In 2006, Ekonomist magazine named him Entrepreneur of the Year, and in the same year, he received the Businessman of the Year award from Istanbul University’s Faculty of Business Administration and Dünya Newspaper.
In 2007, the Health Volunteers Association recognized him as the individual who made the most significant voluntary contributions to healthcare as part of the Social Responsibility Awards in Healthcare. In 2009, he was presented with the TAF Special Award at the 4th Turkish-Arab Forum. In 2010, he was awarded the Grand National Assembly of Turkey’s Distinguished Service Medal and Certificate in recognition of his contributions to the country.
In 2010, he received the Namık Sevik Special Award from Milliyet Newspaper and was included in Capital Magazine’s Most Generous Businesspeople in Turkey list for two consecutive years, ranking 5th in 2014. In 2013, Boğaziçi University, one of Turkey’s most prestigious educational institutions, named him Businessman of the Year, and in 2015, he was honored with the Lifetime Achievement Award by the same university.
Beyond his business and philanthropic achievements, Mehmet Ali Aydınlar has made significant contributions to sports and civil society organizations. He served as a board member of Fenerbahçe Sports Club for five years and held the presidency of the Healthcare Institutions Association for two terms. He is also the President of the Association of Accredited Hospitals and has served two terms on the Board of Directors of TÜSİAD, the Turkish Industry and Business Association. Additionally, he was the 64th President of the Turkish Football Federation. Since 2015, he has been a Board Member of DEİK (Foreign Economic Relations Board of Turkey).
Devamı
Board of Trustees
Mehmet Ali Aydınlar
Chairman of Board of Trustees

Additionally, he was the 64th President of the Turkish Football Federation. Since 2015, he has been a Board Member of DEİK (Foreign Economic Relations Board of Turkey).
Devamı
Seher Aydınlar
Member of the Board of Trustees
After graduating from the Faculty of Chemistry at Galatasaray University, Seher Aydınlar entered the healthcare sector in 1993 by joining Acıbadem Hospital. She currently serves as the Vice-Chair of the Board of Directors at Acıbadem Healthcare Group.
Tahsin Güney
Member of the Board of Trustees
Tahsin Güney graduated from Eskişehir Atatürk High School in 1984 and earned his bachelor's degree in Public Administration from Middle East Technical University (METU) in 1989. In 1999, he completed a Master’s degree in Actuarial Science and Statistics at City University London.
Between 1990 and 2000, he worked as an Inspector at the Social Insurance Institution (Sosyal Sigortalar Kurumu). In 2000, he was appointed as the founding Director of the Finance and Actuarial Department within the same institution. From 2005 to 2008, he served as Deputy President, Acting President, and Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors at the Social Security Institution (SGK), actively contributing to the country’s social security reform efforts.
In 2008, Güney joined Acıbadem Healthcare Group as the Director of Planning and Business Development. In 2013, he was appointed Deputy General Manager of Regional Operations, and in March 2020, he took on the role of General Manager of Acıbadem Healthcare Group.
Devamı
Prof. Dr. Bülent SOYUPAK
Member of the Board of Trustees
Prof. Dr. Bülent Soyupak graduated from Tarsus American College in 1981 and went on to earn his medical degree from Istanbul University Cerrahpaşa Faculty of Medicine in 1987. Between 1988 and 1993, he served as a research assistant in the Department of Urology at Çukurova University Faculty of Medicine.
During this period, he spent three months as an observer at Georgetown University Hospital. In 1995, he joined the Department of Urology at the University of Pennsylvania as a Visiting Faculty Member. Upon returning to Turkey in 1996, he resumed his academic career at Çukurova University Faculty of Medicine, Department of Urology, where he was awarded the title of Associate Professor in 1998 and Professor in 2005. Since 2012, Prof. Dr. Soyupak has been serving as the Medical Director and Chief Physician at Acıbadem Adana Hospital. He is married and has two children.
Devamı
Birol YÜCEL
Member of the Board of Trustees
Birol Yücel is a founding partner of Map Yatırım, a venture capital platform. With over 25 years of experience in the financial sector, he has held various leadership roles, including Auditor and Consultant at Arthur Andersen, Deputy General Manager responsible for Financial Affairs at Yapı ve Kredi Bank, and Partner and Manager of the Turkven Real Estate Fund. Throughout his career, he has taken on board-level responsibilities across multiple industries, including finance, real estate, telecommunications, tourism, healthcare, and energy.
Gökhan GÜRCAN
Member of the Board of Trustees
Gökhan Gürcan was appointed CEO of Bupa Acıbadem Sigorta in 2010 and, as of 2021, serves as the Chairman of the Board while also acting as the interim General Manager. He has been a member of the Acıbadem University Board of Trustees since 2020.
Throughout his tenure, he has successfully navigated the company through critical transitions, including its acquisitions by Khazanah in 2013 and Bupa in 2019. Under his leadership, Bupa Acıbadem Sigorta has introduced groundbreaking services and products, positioning itself as a pioneering and leading company in the health insurance sector.
With 25 years of experience in the insurance industry, Gürcan has held various leadership positions at Allianz and Ergo. He is a graduate of Harvard’s GMP and Koç University’s MDP programs. Additionally, he holds an MBA from Yeditepe University and a bachelor’s degree in Management Information Systems and Business Administration from Marmara University.
Devamı
Ahmet Sedat ARTUKOĞLU
Member of the Board of Trustees
Born in Istanbul in 1964, he graduated from Galatasaray High School in 1983 and completed his studies at the Department of Economics, Faculty of Economic and Administrative Sciences, Marmara University in 1987.
In 2004, he completed the Health Institutions Management Master's Program at the Marmara University Institute of Health Sciences.
Since starting his career in 1989, he has held various management positions. He worked for 4 years at International Hospital-Istanbul, most recently serving as the Marketing Manager. After 1.5 years as the Assistant General Manager at German Hospital, he worked as a consultant in the healthcare sector for 4 years.
In 1997, he started working at Acıbadem Hospital as the General Coordinator. He has held various administrative roles within Acıbadem Healthcare Group. During this time, he worked as a responsible manager on special projects such as corporate development, public offering, and matters related to the group’s investments. In 2004, he became a founding partner of Acıbadem Project Management Inc., which began operations under Acıbadem Holding. He currently serves as the General Manager of Acıbadem Project Management and as a member of the Executive Committee of Acıbadem Healthcare Group.
Devamı
Prof. Dr. Ahmet Şahin
Member of the Board of Trustees
Prof. Dr. Ahmet Şahin graduated from Istanbul Galatasaray High School in 1979 and earned his medical degree from Hacettepe University Faculty of Medicine in 1985. He completed his residency in the Department of Urology at Hacettepe University Faculty of Medicine in 1991, attaining the title of Associate Professor in 1995 and full Professorship in 2002. Between 1996 and 1997, he furthered his expertise by working at the Urology Clinic of Long Island Jewish Hospital in the United States for one year.
From 2000 to 2008, he held key administrative roles at Hacettepe Adult Hospital, serving as Deputy Chief Physician and later as Chief Physician. In 2008, he joined Acıbadem Healthcare Group, where he served as Director of Medical Services from 2008 to 2012. Since May 2012, he has been the Chairman of the Medical Executive Board at Acıbadem Healthcare Group, and as of August 2012, he has been serving as the Rector of Acıbadem University.
Devamı
Rector
Prof. Dr. Ahmet Şahin
Rector
Dear Students,
Acıbadem University is a young and thematic institution, dedicated to the field of health sciences. Since its establishment, two powerful driving forces have fueled its growth and progress.
The first is the desire to pass on the wealth of knowledge and expertise gained through Acıbadem Healthcare Group’s longstanding commitment to providing high-quality healthcare services. The second is the passion of the distinguished academics within our group, who are eager to contribute to health sciences through research and by educating future generations of professionals.
Our university’s primary mission is to cultivate well-informed, inquisitive, and research-oriented professionals in every area of healthcare through student-centered, innovative, and contemporary educational programs.
In pursuit of this goal, we place great emphasis on building a strong educational infrastructure that meets global standards. A prime example of this is our state-of-the-art simulation center, which offers students an invaluable learning experience by allowing them to engage in a wide array of clinical scenarios before encountering real patients. We are well aware that the standards in healthcare education have been redefined, and Acıbadem has set the bar higher than ever before.
We persistently pursue every innovation that enhances education, and we will continue to do so. Learning what we do not yet know is essential to us, but we are already experts in teaching what we know best. Our journey toward excellence will never cease; with strength, confidence, and unwavering hope, we will always look toward the future.
Devamı

Acıbadem Health and Education Foundation was founded on May 28th, 2002.
Foundation was founded to:
Contribute to increase our country’s level of education and healthy living conditions;
Found and operate University, Special Education Academies, Hospitals and Health Facilities and Exclusive Specialty Institutes;
Educate qualified health professionals;
Contribute to the education of students in health field by in-cash and in-kind support;
Conduct necessary researches that would enable health institutions to provide more productive services as well as to create projects in this field and put them in action.
In accordance with this purpose, Foundation founded Acıbadem University, in 2007. Besides providing scholarship for many students studying at Acıbadem University, the Foundation also has been shedding light on science by supporting academic personnel’s scientific work (national and international congress attendances and publications), since it was founded.

Click here to access KAV website.
KEREM AYDINLAR FOUNDATION
PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING INFORMATION NOTICE
a) Data Controller and Its Representative
Pursuant to the Law on the Protection of Personal Data No. 6698 (“Law No. 6698”), your personal data may be processed by Kerem Aydınlar Foundation (“KAV”) as the data controller, within the scope explained below.
b) Purposes for Which Personal Data Will Be Processed
Your personal data collected may be processed for the purposes of ensuring that you benefit from the services offered by KAV; evaluating and finalizing applications of students who apply for scholarships; carrying out scholarship-related activities; increasing awareness and visibility of ongoing projects; customizing the services offered by KAV in line with individuals’ needs and presenting or promoting them to the relevant persons; planning and executing the necessary activities for classifying individuals based on their characteristics, education, and talents; and analyzing such data in line with the objectives set forth in KAV’s charter, including processing by third parties providing services for these purposes.
Your personal data may be processed within the scope of the personal data processing conditions and purposes specified under Articles 5 and 6 of Law No. 6698. If the reasons requiring the storage of your personal data cease to exist and/or if there is no legal basis to retain them for a longer period, such data will be deleted or anonymized.
c) Persons to Whom Personal Data May Be Transferred and the Purposes of Such Transfer
Your personal data collected may be shared —for the purposes stated above— with our suppliers, business partners, Acıbadem Healthcare Group companies, public institutions and organizations, and third-party service providers.
d) Method and Legal Basis of Collecting Personal Data
Your personal data are processed through various channels, both physically and electronically, primarily within the scope of the activities carried out by KAV, for the purposes specified in this information notice and for fulfilling our related legal obligations.
e) Security of Your Personal Data
KAV takes all necessary technical and administrative measures to ensure an appropriate level of security to prevent the unlawful processing of your data, unauthorized access to your data, and to ensure their protection.
f) Your Rights Under Article 11 of Law No. 6698
You have the right to request information from KAV regarding whether your personal data are processed, and if processed, to request information in this regard; to learn the purpose of processing and whether the data are used in accordance with such purpose; to learn the third parties to whom the data are transferred domestically or abroad; to request the correction of the data if they are incomplete or inaccurate; to request the deletion or destruction of personal data within the scope of the applicable legislation; to request that such correction/deletion/destruction be notified to third parties to whom the data have been transferred; to object to a result arising against you as a consequence of analysis of your personal data exclusively through automated systems; and to request compensation if you suffer damages due to unlawful processing of your personal data.
You also have the right to request that communication with you be blocked. Such requests may be submitted to KAV at the following address, addressed to the attention of the “Board of Trustees”: “İçerenköy, Kayışdağı Cd. No:32, 34684 Ataşehir/İstanbul, Türkiye.”

Our Quality Assurance Policy
Our university has adopted a "quality-oriented" management system with an understanding that prioritizes efficiency and sustainable excellence in the services it offers in the fields of education, research, administrative processes, community service, and entrepreneurship.
Fundamental Principles of Our Quality Assurance Policy
Adoption of the quality assurance system by leaders and staff
Maximum stakeholder satisfaction
Strategic management model
Continuation of regular review and improvement efforts
Our Education Policy
In order to contribute to humanity, our university has adopted an education policy that aims to train professionals who:
Think critically, question, generate ideas, research, use technology effectively, keep up with the information age, act with team spirit, adhere to ethical values, respect people and the environment, are creative, know foreign languages, have high self-confidence, and lead in their fields,
and which is:
A model at the national level
Recognized internationally
Focused on continuous learning and development
Our Distance Education Policy
To implement up-to-date, student-centered and blended education methods that are shareable, sustainable, traceable, and measurable, even under challenging conditions, in line with the university’s mission, vision, education policy, and the requirements of the era, by utilizing the digital opportunities offered by technology.
Our Research and Development Policy
To develop an appropriate institutional culture and supportive environment for research that will contribute to universal science in the fields of health, life, and social sciences, and biotechnology
To set strategies aimed at becoming a globally recognized university through research that contributes to universal science, supports entrepreneurship and innovation, by following international developments and innovations in science and technology
To update strategies aimed at sustainability and development through continuous monitoring of the university’s research processes and outputs
To support entrepreneurship with internal and external stakeholders for transforming discoveries resulting from research in health, life and social sciences, and biotechnology into products
Click to access the Strategic Goals of Research and Development
Click to access the Research and Development Priority Areas
Our Social Contribution Policy
To train socially responsible professionals who are sensitive to individual and cultural differences, aware of societal conditions and problems, and focused on service and solutions, through community-oriented and in-community education
To support social responsibility projects carried out within the community through curriculum-based courses and projects, as well as the voluntary activities of student clubs
To encourage collaborations between external stakeholders, faculty members, and students within the scope of community service
To ensure that all units’ research, development, and educational activities contribute to society; and to offer the products of advanced technology and human resources, especially in priority areas such as health, to the service of humanity
To contribute to the scientific future of Turkey, society, and humanity by supporting innovative and entrepreneurial ideas at the Incubation Center
Click to access the Strategic Goals of Social Contribution
Principles of the Human Resources Personnel Polic
(1) The following principles shall be observed in the implementation of the University's human resources personnel policy:
a) Giving priority to the principles related to the University’s working procedures and the expertise required by the position in the selection of personnel to be employed,
b) Respecting the employee’s personality, protecting and safeguarding their rights,
c) Ensuring that employees work in positions suitable to their knowledge and abilities,
d) Establishing working conditions that enhance service quality in line with the University’s objectives,
e) Developing systems that encourage and reward success,
f) Taking measures to promote cooperation, solidarity, and motivation among University employees,
g) Regulating, developing, and supervising the employee’s sense of responsibility, work discipline, attitude, and behavior required by the position.
These principles are adopted as the
“Human Resources Policy.”
Our Internationalization Policy
To increase international recognition and enhance international collaborations in education, teaching, and research
To strengthen the university’s international impact
To encourage international exchange programs and visits for students, academic staff, researchers, and administrative personnel
To become a center of attraction for international students, highly qualified researchers, and faculty members
To guide students for international careers
To ensure international acceptance of educational programs
To create a multicultural and international campus environment and to develop intercultural awareness and competencies among internal stakeholders
Click to access the Strategic Goals of Internationalization
Our Information Security Policy
This policy covers our university’s academic and administrative activities, the information assets related to these activities, and the relevant business processes carried out to protect these assets within the scope of information security.
Our Information Security Policy entails:
Managing the Information Security Management System in accordance with ISO 27001:2022
Ensuring the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information by protecting our information assets
Maintaining and improving the level of information security
Evaluating and managing risks that may occur on information assets through risk management practices
Complying with legal regulations regarding information security, taking necessary measures promptly in the event of security breaches, and applying appropriate sanctions when necessary
Conducting awareness-raising activities and providing training for all our stakeholders regarding information security
Committing to provide the necessary resources and training for the implementation and continuity of this policy
Our Gender Equality Policy
Principles have been defined at our university to ensure that the academic, professional, and social environment is completely free from gender-based discrimination and violence. Within this scope, a framework has been established to support awareness, prevention, support, and empowerment efforts, and infrastructure has been developed to contribute to the processes that will be implemented in the face of gender-based discrimination and violence.
Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI) Policy
Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University is committed to fostering an academic, professional and social environment grounded in diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI). The University recognizes diversity as a core institutional strength and actively works to eliminate discrimination, structural barriers and inequalities across all areas of university life.
This policy document shows the university's determination to carry out its education, research, and societal contribution activities by considering this commitment. In line with this policy, any employee, student or visitor should be treated as such regardless of race, color, gender identity, sexual orientation or ethnicity. The policy endorses the prevention of harassment or discrimination against any person based on gender identity, national origin, religion, age or disability.
The University's DEI Policy and its implementation must be consistent with the Gender-Based Discrimination, Violence and Sexual Harassment Prevention and Support Policy. This latter policy is essential for defining the principles that guide the complete elimination of gender-based discrimination and violence in all aspects—academic, professional, and social—of Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University.
Institutional Commitment
The University adopts a zero-tolerance approach to discrimination, harassment, bullying and victimization. Equality and inclusion principles are embedded in governance, education, research, employment, student life and external engagement, in line with national legislation and international frameworks, including the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
The University is committed to treat all employees, students, and visitors with respect regardless of race, color, gender identity, sexual orientation, or ethnicity. Furthermore, the University prohibits harassment or discrimination against any person based on gender identity, national origin, religion, age, or disability.
Scope and Coverage
This policy applies to:
Academic and administrative staff
Students and applicants
Visiting scholars, contractors, partners and visitors
It covers all university-related contexts, including recruitment, promotion, education, research, campus life, digital environments and institutional representation.
Equity and Equal Opportunity
Acıbadem University ensures fair, transparent and merit-based processes in recruitment, promotion, career progression and student admissions. Proactive measures are implemented to support underrepresented and disadvantaged groups and to promote gender balance at all levels.
Gender Equality and Intersectionality
Gender equality is a strategic priority. The University implements a Gender Equality Plan (GEP) aligned with European and international standards, addressing leadership balance, career development, work-life balance, inclusive governance and gender-sensitive research and education. An intersectional approach recognizes that individuals may experience multiple and overlapping forms of disadvantage.
Accessibility and Inclusion
The University is committed to providing accessible physical, digital and learning environments and to making reasonable adjustments for disabled staff and students. Inclusive teaching practices and curriculum design are promoted to reflect diverse perspectives and learning needs.
Governance and Accountability
Overall responsibility for DEI lies with the Rectorate, supported by the Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Council, which includes academic, administrative and student representatives. The Council oversees implementation, monitors progress and advises on institutional priorities. University’s Gender Equality Commission (TOCEK) works in close collaboration with the DEI Council.
Monitoring and Transparency
The University systematically collects and analyses anonymized equality data relating to staff and students. Progress is reviewed regularly and reported to governing bodies to ensure accountability and continuous improvement.
Safe Reporting and Complaints
Clear, confidential and fair procedures are in place for staff and students to report discrimination, harassment or misconduct. Retaliation against individuals who raise concerns in good faith is not acceptable.
Continuous Improvement
This policy is reviewed periodically to reflect evolving legal requirements, institutional priorities and best international practice, ensuring that diversity, equity and inclusion remain integral to Acıbadem University’s mission and values.
Tobacco-Free Campus Policy
Purpose
The purpose of this Policy is to establish a healthy, safe, and sustainable environment for students, employees, and visitors on the University campus; to reduce health risks associated with the use of tobacco products; to support environmental sustainability; and to strengthen the University’s alignment with national and international health policies, in line with the acception of health promotion as an institutional priority at Acibadem University.
In line with its commitment to social responsibility, the University recognizes the promotion of a healthy lifestyle culture and the development of a campus model compatible with sustainable development goals as an institutional obligation.
Rationale
The World Health Organization (WHO) identifies tobacco use as one of the leading preventable causes of death and highlights its adverse impacts on health, social well-being, and environmental sustainability. In Türkiye, tobacco use—particularly among young populations—remains at levels that require significant public health attention.
The WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) provides a framework that promotes smoke-free public environments and the strengthening of education and awareness activities, while supporting international human rights standards such as the right to health and the right to a safe living environment.
Within the scope of the United Nations 2030 Agenda, the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) encourage the strengthening of tobacco control measures. SDG 4 recognizes healthy learning environments as a prerequisite for quality education, while SDG 12 and SDG 13 support efforts to reduce the environmental impacts of tobacco production and waste.
As a health-oriented university, and in line with our institutional approach that recognizes health as a fundamental human right, establishing a safe, healthy, and sustainable campus environment is one of our primary responsibilities. This Policy will serve protecting the right to health and to establish an institutional framework aligned with international obligations.
Scope
This Policy applies to all University campuses, including all indoor and outdoor areas, buildings, dormitories, social facilities, sports facilities, parking areas, and all vehicles owned or operated by the University. It also applies to all tobacco products, Electronic Nicotine Delivery Systems (ENDS) which include, but are not limited to, electronic cigarettes (e-cigarettes), vape devices, vaporizers, vape pens, e-cigars, e-pipes, and all similar electronic devices designed for nicotine delivery, and Electronic Non-Nicotine Delivery Systems (ENNDS), which include electronic devices that do not contain nicotine but are similar to ENDS in terms of their method of use, appearance, and aerosol exposure, and that are used through inhalation.
Policy Principles
The use of tobacco products, ENDS, and ENNDS is not acceptable within University campus boundaries, except in areas designated by the University administration.
This Policy applies to all students, academic and administrative staff, subcontracted employees, and visitors.
During the transition period towards achieving a tobacco-free campus, designated smoking areas shall be established on campus.
All University events (including congresses, seminars, ceremonies, and similar activities) shall be organized as tobacco-free events.
Supportive Measures
Smoking cessation counseling and referral services shall be provided.
Informative and awareness-raising educational activities shall be conducted.
Visible warning and information signs shall be installed throughout the campus.
Implementation and Monitoring
The implementation of this Policy shall be carried out under the coordination of the Rectorate by the relevant units and/or committees. The effectiveness of the Policy shall be evaluated at regular intervals, and necessary improvements shall be made.
The procedures and principles regarding the implementation of this Policy shall be regulated through the Tobacco-Free Campus Implementation Directive to be issued by the University.

Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University, was established as a foundation university with a legal personality by Acıbadem Health and Education Foundation, depending on the Additional Article 74 added to the Law on the Organization of Higher Education Institutions dated 28 March 1983 and numbered 2809, with the 1st article of the Law numbered 5656 published in the Official Gazette dated May 18, 2007 and numbered 26526.
In the 2009-2010 academic year, in its first temporary campus located in Maltepe Gülsuyu, it started education activities by taking first students to the seven programs in School of Medicine, Nursing, Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation, Health Management Departments of Faculty of Health Sciences and Health Services Vocational School.
In the following years, Departments of Psychology, Molecular Biology & Genetics, Sociology of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Engineering, Department of Medical Engineering, Faculty of Pharmacy, Biomedical Device Technology and Cookery Programs of the Vocational School, other programs of the Vocational School of Health Services and Health Sciences, Science and Social Sciences Institutes, Senology Research Institute became operational and associate, undergraduate and graduate programs were opened.
The name of Acıbadem University was changed to Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University with the law numbered 7033, published in the Official Gazette dated July 01, 2017 and numbered 30111, on commemorating the founder of the University, Acıbadem Health and Education Foundation, and the Chairman of the Board of Trustees Mehmet Ali Aydınlar.

Vision
To be a pioneer and innovator in education, research and service in health, life and social sciences, one of the best universities in Turkey and one of the leading universities in the world.
Mission
To raise leaders who are competent in their field, culturally and socially equipped, sensitive to society and the environment, have a lifelong learning habit, and attach importance to ethical values,
To provide contemporary, innovative and pioneering education and research environments based on Kemalist thought, to contribute to society and humanity in this way,
To work to achieve the perfect level in education, research and service by prioritizing scientific thought at the forefront.
Core Values
Scientific Freedom
Freedom of Thought
Justice
Commitment to Ethical Values
Respect for Diversity
Innovativeness
Perfectionism
Participation
Transparency
Devotion to Reason and Knowledge
Professionalism

Quality Assurance Policy
Our university has adopted a "quality-oriented" management system with the main understanding of efficiency and sustainable excellence in the services it provides in the fields of education, research, administrative processes, community service, and entrepreneurship.
Quality Assurance Policy Core Values
Quality assurance system adopted by leaders and employees
The highest level of stakeholder satisfaction
Strategic management model
Sustaining regular review and improvement studies
Education Policies
Our university aims to contribute to humanity; We, as Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University and be in an attempt to contribute to humanity have adopted an education policy that is:
a model at national level
referenced globally
focused on continuous learning and development
with the aim of raising professionals who think, question, produce ideas, research, use technology well, keep up with the information age, act with team spirit, adhere to ethical values, respect people and the environment, creative, speak foreign languages, have high self-confidence and pioneer in their field, we have adopted an education policy that is
Distance Education Policy
Application of student-centered and hybrid up-to-date education methods that are compatible with the mission, vision, education policy of our university and the needs of the age, and that can be shared, maintained, monitored and measured by using the digital opportunities offered by technology even under difficult circumstances.
Research and Development Policy
To develop an appropriate corporate culture and supportive environment for research that will contribute to universal science in the fields of health, life and social sciences and biotechnology.
To determine strategies to become an internationally recognized university with research that will contribute to universal science by following international developments and innovations in the field of science and technology and support entrepreneurship and innovation.
To determine strategies to become an internationally recognized university prominent for research that will contribute to universal science by following international developments and innovations in the field of science and technology and support entrepreneurship and innovation.
To update sustainability and development strategies by constantly monitoring the university's research processes and outputs
To support entrepreneurship with internal and external stakeholders in order to transform the inventions emerged through research in the fields of health, life and social sciences and biotechnology into products.
Research-Development Priority Areas
Neuroscience
Biotechnology
a. Biomaterials and Tissue engineering
b. Medical Biotechnology
c. Pharmaceutical Biotechnology
2. Cancer Research
3. Rare Diseases
4. Biomedical Research
a. Medical Device
b. Biomedical Imaging
c. Computational fields (Bioinformatics)
5. Health Policies 6. Fundamental Science Research
Social Contribution Policy
To educate professionals who are sensitive to individual and cultural differences and social problems, who are conscious of social conditions and problems, who are responsible, who are service and solution-oriented,
To support the volunteer activities of student clubs as well as carrying out social responsibility projects realized in the community through courses and projects within the scope of the curriculum,
To encourage faculty members’ and students’ collaborations with external stakeholders, within the scope of community service,
To aim to transform the research, development and training activities carried out by all units into social contributions; offering the products of its advanced technology and human resources to the service of humanity in the fields of research for the priority problems of the society, especially health,
To be a higher education institution that contributes to Turkey's scientific future, society and humanity by supporting innovative and entrepreneurial ideas in the Incubation Center.
Our Internationalization Policy
To increase international recognition, increasing international cooperation in the fields of education, training and research
To strenghten the University's international influence
To encourage international exchange programs and visits of students, academic staff, researchers and administrative staff
To become a center of attraction for international students, highly qualified researchers and faculty
To guide students for international careers
To ensure international acceptance of educational programs
To create a multicultural and international campus environment and to cultivate the intercultural sensitivity and competencies of internal stakeholders

ACADEMIC AUTONOMY AND FREEDOM OF EXPRESSION
University; recognizes the importance of promoting and protecting academic freedom in education and scientific research for faculty members.
It is the policy of Acıbadem University to protect and encourage the freedom of teaching staff to express their opinions, to teach, to research and to publish, and to protect them from influences from inside or outside the University that would limit them. Teaching staff; has the freedom to publish research and results, provided that he/she fulfills his/her other academic duties adequately, even if it is an education-oriented assignment, and is subject to the institutional policies and procedures specified in the University's research policies. The external financial gain that the lecturer will obtain after the research carried out should be based on mutual agreement with the authorities of the institution. This process is carried out in accordance with the Directive on Invention, Production and Service Activities at Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University. The instructor is a part of a profession and an educational institution with the identity of an educator. He/She has the freedom to discuss the subject in which he/she is an expert in the classroom environment. When speaking or writing as an individual, he/she should be free from institutional censorship or restrictions, but he/she should know that her position in society comes with certain obligations. The instructor, who is still in the learning process, should keep in mind the influence of what he/she says and writes as a member of an educational institution on forming an opinion by the society about his/her profession and the institution he/she is a member of. Therefore, the instructor should always respect the opinions of others and indicate this when not speaking on behalf of the institution.
ACADEMIC STAFF PRIMARY PROFESSIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES AND DUTIES
The main professional responsibilities and duties of the teaching staff are determined by the 22nd and 31st articles of the Higher Education Law No. 2547.
According to this;
Instructor duties are:
ARTICLE 22
a.
To carry out education, training and applied studies at associate, undergraduate and graduate levels in higher education institutions and to manage project preparations and seminars in accordance with the purposes and principles of this law,
b.
To carry out scientific research and publications in higher education institutions,
c.
To accept students on certain days according to the program to be organized by the head of the relevant unit, helping them in necessary matters, leading and guiding them in line with the purpose and main principles of this law,
d.
To fullfil the duties assigned by the authorized bodies, e. To perform other duties assigned by this law. Instructor duties are:
ARTICLE 31
Lecturers; persons who are known for their studies and works in their fields of expertise can be assigned with a periodical or hourly fee for courses in which there are no lecturers appointed in accordance with this Law in universities and their affiliated units, or for the education, training and practice of any subject that requires special knowledge and expertise.
CONSULTING / MENTORING DUTIES OF TEACHERS:
It is defined in Acıbadem University Associate and Undergraduate Education and Examination Regulations:
ARTICLE 27 – (1) The head of the relevant department assigns an advisor for each student. The advisor monitors the student and advises the student on the compulsory and elective courses that the student should take within the framework of the education programs that the student is continuing, double major-minor programs. During the registration or renewal of the year/semester enrollment, the student organizes his/her lessons together with his/her advisor. In addition, "School of Medicine Mentoring Program" is carried out in the School of Medicine.
School of Medicine Mentoring Program Scope and Definition: Mentoring; is a concept that originated from ages ago. Although used synonymously with faculty advisor in academia, the main difference between mentoring and advising is that mentoring requires a personal relationship as well as a professional one. The mentoring relationship develops over a long period of time. It can change according to the student's needs. The mentor should be aware of these changes and changes, and should be able to adjust his/her attention, help and suggestions, information and motivational incentives accordingly.
A physician candidate may have more than one mentor at different ages, with different equipment and with different personalities during his/her education. He/She derives different benefits from each. The mentor is a role model equipped in his field of expertise, aware of scientific developments and up-to-date information. The mentoring relationship is characterized by mutual respect, trust, understanding and empathy.
Purpose of the Program:
ACUTIP mentoring and peer mentoring system aims to support our faculty students in their medical education process and guide them in their psychosocial development and future career decisions. The mentoring system covers all students studying at our faculty and allows them to communicate with their faculty members and their peers one-on-one.
Mentor's Responsibilities:
To make face-to-face meetings at least twice a year by contacting the student they are mentoring.
To guide the students in the subjects they need in relation to medical education, to transfer their knowledge and experience.
To follow the success and social status of the student at school, to contact the relevant committees through the Mentorship Program Coordinator when necessary.
Being a role model and sharing the medical skills developed over the years in order to prepare the student for life after graduation.
Notifying the Dean's Office in writing in cases where he/she will be interrupted or unable to fulfill his/her mentorship duty for any reason.
Student Responsibilities:
Requesting a meeting with the mentor within one month following the appointment of the mentor lecturer.
Communicate face-to-face with the mentor at least twice a year.
Notifying the Mentor and Mentoring Program Coordinator of contact information changes.
Responsibilities of Peer Mentors:
Getting in touch with the students they mentor.
To guide the students they are peer mentors on the subjects they need, to transfer their own knowledge and experiences.
Meeting face-to-face with students they are peer mentors at least twice a year.
Informing the Mentoring Program Coordinator in writing in case he/she cannot continue her peer mentorship for any reason..
The ACUTIP Mentoring Program Directive contains the basic features and application principles in detail.
TEACHING STAFF - ADMINISTRATIVE PERSONNEL RELATIONS
Today, modern universities need personnel suitable for the nature of the job in order to cope with the ever-changing needs. As universities become more assertive academically, the need for the necessary number and quality of administrative personnel to achieve these goals increases. Acıbadem University administrative staff; consists of tens of talented and qualified employees who give importance to education-training, academic and administrative personnel affairs, research & development and institutional development, have work experience and have trained themselves professionally and individually. Administrative staff; are organized as units or offices in order to support the work of the teaching staff and to provide the best possible service to the students.
Administrative staff at Faculties, Vocational Schools, Institutes and Centers, Academic and Administrative Human Resources Department, Student Affairs Directorate, Financial Affairs Directorate, Library and Electronic Resources Directorate, IT Department, Directorate of Health, Culture and Sports, Corporate Communications Unit, Candidate Relations and Promotion Unit, International Student and Academic Relations Unit, in Laboratories, Dining Hall - Cafeteria Services, Security Unit, Technical and Support Services Unit and in all other units, make the utmost effort to ensure that the business processes are carried out in a timely and healthy manner.
The duties of the administrative staff include; to ensure the coordination and regular execution of all the works of the academic & administrative staff and the students of the Faculty, Vocational School and Institute, to work together with the academic staff to present their suggestions and thoughts within the framework of the legislation in the preparation of the Regulations and Directives, to take an active role in the management of the protocol, press and public relations related jobs and transactions to be made within our university, to forward the unit’s purchase requests of goods and materials to the Rectorate, taking the necessary security measures on the university campus, following up continuous updating of the university web page, the organization of official openings, graduation ceremonies, congresses, conferences, seminars, symposiums, etc. Working together with our instructors in the realization of social and cultural activities, responding to requests from outside the institution, documents and other requests in a timely manner, to ensure that cleaning, maintenance and repair services are carried out regularly, to cooperate with those responsible for the proper functioning of the laboratories, to provide the necessary environment for the sustainability of the working order of the academic staff, to work in harmony with all the employees working at our University, and to provide continuous support to the instructors to ensure that the most research activities, especially education and training, are carried out in the best possible manner. In this respect, administrative staff deserve the respect, appreciation and evaluation of all lecturers due to their self-sacrifice, effort and sense of responsibility.
DISCRIMINATION AND SEXUAL HARASSMENT
Participation in the academic community also entails an obligation to faculty, administrative staff and students to respect the dignity of others, express different views, intellectual honesty, freedom to question and teach, and freedom to promote and defend free expression, on and off campus. Any discriminatory behavior undermines the atmosphere of trust and respect necessary for a healthy academic environment. Therefore, Acıbadem University's policy is to provide an environment free from discrimination based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national or ethnic origin, age, disability or seniority. Sexual harassment includes requests for sexual approach and sexual or gender-based verbal or physical behavior. It may include behavior such as sexual or gender-based unnatural touching or unpleasant behavior (physical, verbal, written or through technology). Acıbadem University is committed to promoting an environment free from all forms of sexual abuse, including sexual assault and sexual harassment. To support this commitment, the University takes steps to raise awareness of such misconduct, eliminate its occurrence on campus, provide support to survivors, diligently investigate all reports of sexual misconduct, and rigorously investigate criminal elements. Creating a safe campus environment and a culture of respect is the shared responsibility of all members of the University, individually and collectively. Acıbadem University recognizes that sexual assault, harassment, discrimination and other forms of sexual abuse can have a profound impact on a person's personal, academic and working life. The University encourages those exposed to such a situation to seek help and support in coping with it.
EMOTIONAL RELATIONS
Acıbadem University is committed to providing an educational environment where learning can flourish. Romantic and sexual relationships between instructors and students, even consensual, negatively affect this environment. Differences in position and power between students and those in positions of teaching, advising, supervising, evaluating, mentoring or mentoring them call into question the student's ability to consciously consent to such relationships. When an instructor and a student have a romantic or sexual relationship, an instructor's credibility in teaching, advising, and evaluating students in an impartial way is undermined. Even if the instructor and student act virtuously, others may perceive it as partially and often biased. The sudden termination of such relationships can have negative effects on the learning environment and harm both the instructor and the student. Sexual or romantic relationships between individuals in naturally unequal positions have the potential for serious breach of trust. Especially when it is realized that this relationship also affects third parties, it can negatively affect the University environment and may extend to accusations such as conflict of interest, nepotism and abuse of power. Often, such relationships end in less reconciliation than is thought. Even when such a relationship ends, there is a risk of bias against the former romantic partner long after the relationship is over.
Sexual or romantic relationships between individuals in naturally unequal positions have the potential for serious breach of trust. Especially when it is realized that this relationship also affects third parties, it can negatively affect the University environment and may extend to accusations such as conflict of interest, nepotism and abuse of power. Often, such relationships end in less reconciliation than is thought. Even when such a relationship ends, there is a risk of bias against the former romantic partner long after the relationship is over.
Due to the potential vulnerability of undergraduates in such relationships, sexual or romantic relationships between faculty and undergraduate students are not tolerated in any way. If an instructor has a previous sexual or romantic relationship with a student, the instructor should not only release any responsibility for counseling on that student, but also immediately inform their supervisor so that different educational or counseling arrangements can be made.
DISCIPLINE ISSUES AND JUST TERMINATION
The University may terminate the contract unilaterally by the University if the instructor violates the obligations defined in Articles 4 and 5 of the Instructor Contract or other commitments specified in this contract, or if it causes one of the following conditions.
As a result of the disciplinary investigation to be opened in accordance with the 53rd article of the Higher Education Law No. 2547, he was sentenced to dismissal from duty,
Behaviors and statements against the Founding Foundation or the University,
Making a statement to the press, other than scientific matters related to his field of duty, and making written or oral explanations, without the written permission of the Rectorate,
The Instructor shall not disclose any information to third parties about the affairs, accounts, financial situations, professional secrets, activities, agreements, transactions or interests of the University, ASG or subsidiaries or their owners and managers, which he knows about or learned during his studies, without the written permission of the University, and he/she accepts and undertakes not to allow the disclosure of the information and not to use them even personally. The employment contract of the academic staff who are found to act in violation of this condition will be terminated immediately.
A faculty member cannot leave his/her duty during the contract period for any reason, except for attending congresses and conferences, sick leave based on the report to be obtained from official health institutions, and other permissions obtained from the Rector's Office. The University may terminate the contract without compensation if the instructor does not come to his/her duty without an excuse and without prior notice, and the absence is up to "3" days each time and this absence is repeated three times within the contract period. If the absence of the instructor exceeds 3 days at a time, it is the reason for the termination of the contract without compensation.

Full-time Faculty Member
Acıbadem University faculty members are expected to act in line with the University's vision and mission in the fields of education, research and service to society and the institution. Taking part in these fields is the responsibility of the instructor towards the students and the institution. In a rapidly developing university, academic and administrative managerial duties are also within the scope of the teaching staff. Although each academic unit (Faculty, Vocational School, etc.) has its own different applications, these responsibilities include all full-time lecturers.
Educational Responsibilities: Lecturers are obliged to give lectures in accordance with the academic program determined by the unit they are affiliated with in both semesters, except for the periods when official leave is taken, congress permits or administrative responsibilities. Course loads may differ between instructors and from year to year. The educational responsibility of the instructor is determined and announced at the beginning of each academic year by the Dicipline/Major Field of Study/Program/Department together with the Dean's Office/Directorate.
In terms of educator responsibility towards students, every lecturer takes equal responsibility regardless of title/seniority. Entry-level subjects do not need to be taught by junior lecturers, and more advanced courses by senior lecturers. From the first years of undergraduate education, students should be in contact with all distinguished and experienced instructors. Course loads can be determined by considering the course hours in the program and the research goals of the current staff in that academic year. The instructor who wants to spend more time on research can take less course responsibility with the approval of the department. Academics are expected to balance education and research together. Small group training, bedside training, case discussions can be alternatives to lectures, especially for the faculty members working in the clinical departments of the School of Medicine, who are responsible for serving in a hospital environment, in order to fulfill their educational duties in a way that does not interfere with clinical service. Instructors are expected to offer elective courses in their fields of interest/expertise. It is the extra-curricular responsibilities of the instructors to form a Student Research Group that students can apply to independently and to participate in the Mentoring program.
Availability of Faculty Members: The academic staff of the university undertakes to comply with the curriculum and laboratory hours determined at the beginning of the academic year, to participate in the administrative meetings of the department and faculty/vocational school and to fulfill the duties given in the commissions and committees. All lecturers are expected to be available to meet with students on fixed days and times, either regularly or by appointment. It is important that faculty members are also accessible to other academics.
Instructors cannot change the course hours and days determined at the beginning of the academic year without a valid excuse and without prior notice. Courses that are cancelled this way are added to the program as soon as possible. Instructors are responsible for preparing questions for the exams, sending these questions within the specified time, and taking part in the jury in the oral exams.
Research Responsibilities: Full-time faculty members are expected to continue their research and publish in the fields of science, in addition to their educational roles. The time devoted to research may differ among instructors. Considering different responsibilities, some instructors may carry out more research and publications in the same Major Field of Study/Department. Service Responsibilities: Taking part in the management of education, boards and commissions or in administrative duties is considered as a service to the institution. The course load of these instructors can be reduced. Duties within professional organizations and non-governmental organizations are evaluated under the heading of community service.
Full-time teaching staff, who are expected to operate in the fields of education, research and service, covers all academics registered in YÖKSİS, whose assignment procedures have been completed within the framework of the YÖK Law. They are responsible for keeping their information in the AVESIS database up to date. They are included in the Periodic Academic Performance Evaluation (PAPD) process. In this context, the periodic evaluation of each faculty member is carried out by the Dean's Office/Directorate by taking into account the previous calendar year in RESEARCH field; the academic year completed in the fields of EDUCATION/SERVICE TO THE INSTITUTION/SOCIETY and ACHIEVEMENTS & RECOGNITION.
RESEARCHER ACADEMIC STAFF
Independent researcher / project-laboratory manager includes basic scientist or clinician lecturers.
The contract period of the Research Faculty Member is made for a certain period of not less than three years and not more than five years. Contract renewal is subject to satisfactory performance as determined by members of the centre, institute, laboratory or department as appropriate. It is also necessary to have external funds available to support research. It is expected that the lecturer who starts in the doctoral faculty staff will receive the title of associate professor within six years and meet the criteria for promotion. The contract of the doctor lecturer who does not meet this will not be renewed.
Research: The main task of a research lecturer is to conduct research on the academic program of the department, center, institute or laboratory where he works. The research program may also include students, research assistants, other faculty and staff, and may be conducted on or off campus due to the nature of the research.
Research Funds: Research faculty members are responsible for providing external funding to support research programs. Researchers at all levels can also provide funding through the research fund, which is subject to the approval and monitoring of faculty management, center or institute director, department head and university management.
Supervision of Student Research: Research teaching staff can carry out undergraduate and graduate research projects subject to the approval of the faculty and center/institute; in accordance with the graduate education policy he/she can also serve on thesis committees.
Education: He/She can teach at undergraduate and especially graduate level. Such cases are taken into account in the periodic evaluation of the research instructor.
Service responsibilities: Research faculty members may serve on boards and commissions in a way that does not cause a conflict of interest in terms of research funding, although they do not have basic duties. They have the right to attend the meetings of the department they are affiliated with and to vote in the academic board.
LECTURER
Persons who are known for their studies and works in their fields of expertise can be assigned for the education, training and practice of any subject that requires special knowledge and expertise with a periodic or course hour fee for the courses that do not have a faculty member appointed in accordance with the Law in universities and their affiliated units. The primary responsibility of lecturers is education. Can be appointed up to two years initially and this period is renewable.
VISITING FACULTY MEMBER
There is no YÖKSİS record. There is an AVESIS record. His/Her name is on the university website. A business card is printed. He/She benefits from the University Library and digital resources. An e-mail account with the
edu.tr
extension is provided by the university. He/She is not subject to the Periodic Academic Performance Evaluation process. He/She is expected to operate in the fields of education and/or research. It is obligatory to use the University address for publications in Incites Q1-Q2-Q3 categories. Incites Q4 category publications should use non-university (hospital etc.) addresses. Guest Faculty Member status is re-approved by the relevant Dean's Office / Directorate at the beginning of each academic year.

pages.title: PAPD Process | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: Each academic staff member is expected to regularly record and keep up to date all academic activities—primarily their publications—in AVESİS.The 2024 PAPD calendar and implementation principles approved by the Board of Trustees are announced to all academics by the Office of the Rector.2024 PAPD Process:
pages.text:
Home
University
Home
University
PAPD Process
Periodic Academic Performance Evaluation (PAPD) Process
PAPD Policy
Rectorate PAPD Commission
PAPD Scope
PAPD Process
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
Each academic staff member is expected to regularly record and keep up to date all academic activities—primarily their publications—in AVESİS.
The 2024 PAPD calendar and implementation principles approved by the Board of Trustees are announced to all academics by the Office of the Rector.
2024 PAPD Process:
1.
Application, evaluation, announcement, and objection procedures are carried out through the Acıbadem University Academic Performance Evaluation Process Management System (APSİS), accessible at
http://apsis.acibadem.edu.tr/
, in accordance with the PAPD calendar announced by the Office of the Rector.
2.
Academics are required to add the activities they will use in their applications to their personal AVESİS profiles.
3.
During the application phase, academics’ activities are retrieved from AVESİS by APSİS and transferred into the application field. Self-evaluation is also completed in APSİS by filling out the relevant fields. However, any activities not defined in AVESİS, as well as additional data requested by the Dean’s Office/Directorate, must be manually added to the application by academics through APSİS.
4.
It is extremely important that the activities declared in the application are complete and that the explanations regarding self-evaluation and goals are comprehensive enough to include all types of professional activities (excluding clinical service) that the academic has carried out, worked on, or devoted time to during the relevant period. This allows the Dean/Director reviewing the applications to conduct a holistic and complete evaluation of the academic’s activities without the need for verbal communication.
5.
The Dean’s Office/Directorate reviews the applications and communicates any requests for additional information/explanations to academics through APSİS.
6.
The Dean’s Office/Directorate obtains the opinions of the program/department/division heads for each academic evaluated.
7.
During the evaluation process, the Dean’s Office/Directorate primarily conducts face-to-face meetings with the following academics. However, if deemed necessary, face-to-face meetings may be conducted with all academics.
a.
Academic staff who have completed their first full calendar year at the University (participating in the PAPD process for the first time)
b.
Academic staff who were evaluated as “Below Expectations” or “Unsatisfactory” in the 2023 PAPD
c.
Assistant professors, lecturers, and research assistants
8.
In the face-to-face meetings conducted with academic staff participating in the PAPD process, the Dean/Director receives information regarding their work, listens to the academic regarding how the institution may further support their research and how their contributions to education and the institution may be improved, and shares their academic evaluation from the previous year as well as expectations for the following year.
9.
The Dean/Director finalizes the evaluation for each academic in APSİS by considering the activities declared in the application, the face-to-face meetings, and the opinions of the program/department/division heads, and evaluates them at the category level (research, education, service to the institution/society, and achievements and recognition) as well as overall performance. Once the Dean/Director completes the evaluations for all academics in the unit, the evaluation report is submitted to the Office of the Rector through APSİS.
10
. The scale to be used in performance evaluations is as follows:
● Outstanding
● Above Expectations
● Successful
● Below Expectations
● Unsatisfactory
11.
The evaluation results submitted by the relevant Dean’s Office/Directorate are reviewed by the Office of the Rector. The Rectorate may request additional information/explanations from academics if necessary. To ensure a standardized approach across the University, it may recommend revisions to the success level evaluations conducted for academics. If revisions are made, they are submitted to the Rectorate via APSİS by the Dean’s Office/Directorate.
12.
The Office of the Rector communicates the performance evaluation results to academics via APSİS.
13.
If academics wish to object to their evaluation results, they may submit their objections—along with justifications and any supporting documents—through APSİS to the Office of the Rector, in accordance with the announced calendar.
14.
The Office of the Rector, in consultation with the relevant Dean/Director, finalizes the objections in accordance with the announced calendar, communicates the final evaluation decisions to academics via APSİS, and concludes the process.
Following the 2024 PAPD Process:
1.
The Board of Trustees, taking into account the performance evaluation results, makes decisions regarding academic staff whose contracts will not be renewed.
2.
Contracts for academic staff deemed eligible for renewal are sent to them by the Human Resources Directorate.
3.
Academic staff whose contracts will be renewed must submit the contract in two copies, signed in wet ink, in a sealed envelope to the Human Resources Directorate as soon as possible.
4.
Throughout the academic year following the completion of the process, the Dean/Director may meet with the academic staff at desired intervals and continue ongoing discussions under any evaluation category when deemed necessary.
OTHER MATTERS
1.
The evaluations of academics who have previously held positions as Rector, Vice Rector, or Dean/Director are conducted by the Dean’s Office/Directorate to which they are affiliated. The evaluations of academics who currently serve as Vice Rectors, Deans, or Directors are conducted by the Office of the Rector.
2.
Academics who have spent the entire year of 2023 on the University’s staff will participate in the 2024 PAPD process.
3.
Failure of academics to participate in the PAPD process will be interpreted as the absence of a request for contract renewal in the following period. Academics who are unable to participate in the PAPD process due to compelling reasons may submit their contract renewal requests—together with their justifications—to their respective Dean’s Office/Directorate by 1 September 2024.
