School of Medicine Medical Education Curriculum Overview
The Acıbadem University Faculty of Medicine Undergraduate Medical Education Program is a comprehensive six-year curriculum designed to educate physicians who possess the competencies required by the medical profession, adhere to ethical principles, and that prioritizes societal health needs. The program is developed in alignment with the National Core Education Program and relevant international strategic frameworks, and is structured with an emphasis on interdisciplinary integration, innovative educational methodologies, and social accountability.
The educational process is grounded in student-centered learning approaches, the principle of social accountability, and the promotion of a strong culture of scientific inquiry. Within this framework, students are expected to actively engage in their learning processes, assume responsibility for their academic and professional development, acquire research and critical thinking skills, and develop an awareness of societal health needs.
Phase I – Preclinical Phase (Years 1–3)
This phase consists of integrated course committees organized around organ systems. Basic and clinical sciences are delivered in an integrated manner. In addition, students acquire knowledge and competencies in ethics, medical humanities, research methodologies, public health, professionalism, and clinical skills.
Phase II – Clinical Phase (Years 4–5)
During this phase, students gain hands-on experience in patient management, differential diagnosis, treatment planning, and participation in healthcare delivery through compulsory and elective clinical clerkships. Clerkships are structured either as discipline-based rotations or as integrated blocks involving related specialties.
Phase III – Internship Phase (Year 6)
The internship phase is conducted based on a “learning through practice” approach. Students actively participate in patient care processes, including diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up, and are expected to achieve the physician competencies defined for undergraduate medical education prior to graduation.
Course Categories
Integrated Medical Courses:
Consist of the Biomedical Sciences Course Committees (BSC) and the Clinical Medicine and Professional Skills (CMPS) programs.
Complementary Medical Courses:
Include compulsory courses (e.g., Medical English, Biostatistics) and elective courses (e.g., Social Responsibility, Medical Language Proficiency).
Common Courses:
University-wide courses offered at the institutional level.
Transition to Clinical Clerkships Program (TCC):
A structured preparatory program designed to facilitate students’ transition to clinical practice.
Clinical Clerkships
: Compulsory and elective clinical rotations conducted during the 4th and 5th years.
Internship Programs: Compulsory and elective clinical practice components implemented throughout the 6th year.

Acıbadem University, Faculty of Medicine Atakent Training and Research Hospital
Established in 2014, the Acıbadem University Atakent Hospital has an indoor area of 60,000 m² and a capacity of 278 beds. The hospital provides healthcare services to an average of 450,000 patients annually.
Delivering healthcare services with a multidisciplinary approach, the hospital hosts numerous departments and specialized centers across a wide range of fields. In addition, it offers comprehensive educational facilities required for the training of future healthcare professionals.
Acıbadem University, Faculty of Medicine Maslak Training and Research Hospital
Established in 2009, the Acıbadem University Maslak Hospital has an indoor area of 106,000 m² and a capacity of 364 beds. The hospital provides healthcare services to an average of 465,000 patients annually.
Delivering healthcare services with a multidisciplinary approach, the hospital hosts numerous departments and specialized centers across a wide range of fields. In addition, it offers comprehensive educational facilities required for the training of future healthcare professionals.

pages.title: Research Goals and Priorities | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: Acıbadem University School of Medicine is committed to educating competent physicians and proficient scientists. As outlined in our Strategic Plan, enhancing the productivity, quality, and impact of research within the Faculty of Medicine is one of our primary goals.

Acıbadem University School of Medicine is committed to educating competent physicians and proficient scientists. As outlined in our
Strategic Plan
, enhancing the productivity, quality, and impact of research within the Faculty of Medicine is one of our primary goals.
In addition to tracking traditional scientific impact, we aim to assess the societal impact of our research. This approach helps us increase the number of studies that meet community needs, lead to applicable products, or influence healthcare policies. To support this goal, we aim to raise the proportion of Q1–Q3 publications from the Faculty of Medicine to over 90%, achieve a Category Normalized Citation Impact (CNCI) of 1.2, increase collaborations with industry, and expand international partnerships.
Over the past five years, the faculty has produced an average of nearly 400 full-length articles or reviews annually, with 5–10% involving student participation. Approximately 60% of these publications appear in Q1 or Q2 journals, and the five-year CNCI is around 1.0, aligning with the global average. Strengthening national and international scientific collaborations remains a top priority to further support these goals.
Within the medical curriculum, student research is integrated into both required and elective courses and is credit-bearing. Beyond the curriculum, students are encouraged to join research groups to enhance their productivity—an important opportunity for those pursuing a career as scientists. Reflecting our students’ high academic potential, we have launched an integrated MD-PhD program, which has already admitted its first cohort.
To support and incentivize faculty research efforts, institutional resources have been established and implemented. These include publication support, statistical assistance, project development support, and more.

ACUMSA
ACUMSA (Acıbadem University Medical Students Association), was established in 2022 through the merger of two medical student organizations operating at Acıbadem University: Acıbadem TÖB (Medical Students’ Union) and EMSA Acıbadem. This merger aimed to bring together different experiences and traditions, enabling medical students to be represented more strongly under a common umbrella.
ACUMSA
ACUTBAT
Since its establishment, ACUTBAT (Acıbadem University School of Medicine Scientific Research Society) has encouraged students to think more, conduct research, and explore. Within the intense pace of medical education, it serves as a guide to help students maintain their passion for science. The interest committees and research groups within our organization enable each student to discover and pursue their own areas of interest.
ACUTBAT
TEÖK
The Medical Education Student Council (TEÖK) is a student organization that enables medical students to actively participate in the planning, implementation, and evaluation processes of the medical education curriculum.

At Acıbadem University Faculty of Medicine, the Pre-Graduation Mentorship Program has been implemented for all students since the establishment of the faculty in order to support their academic, social, and career development. Each student is paired with a faculty member mentor; the mentorship process is carried out not only through individual meetings but also, as of the 2024–2025 academic year, through a hybrid mentorship model that includes group interaction. Within the scope of the program, students’ career planning is supported, their individual development is monitored, and guidance is provided for their academic lives. The mentorship system is an institutionalized support structure that contributes to students’ academic and professional development through regular feedback mechanisms and strong student–faculty interaction. In addition, students’ well-being is supported through a holistic approach via psychological counseling and health services offered within the university.
Mentorship in Medical Education
Mentorship differs from advising; in mentorship, the personal relationship is as important as the professional one, whereas an advisor may or may not be a mentor depending on the nature of the relationship.
Throughout their education, a medical student may have more than one mentor of different ages, backgrounds, and personalities, and benefit from each of them in different ways.
Medical education is a long-term experience. Mentorship supports this process, contributes to social development, and provides guidance for future career decisions.
A good mentor takes a personal interest in the young physician candidate and guides them throughout their professional development process. They generously share the clinical skills and experience they have developed over the years.
A mentor is a role model who is well-equipped in their field of expertise, follows scientific advancements, and possesses up-to-date knowledge. The mentor’s goal is to ensure that the student continually improves both personally and academically.
Effective mentorship requires mutual trust, understanding, and empathy.
Mentors also provide guidance in conducting scientific research and writing academic papers.

Career Development Days of the School of Medicine is a seminar series designed to support our students not only in their professional training but also in their broader career journeys. While the theoretical courses, practical training, and advanced research opportunities offered at our School provide a strong academic foundation, this program aims to enrich our students’ professional development by introducing them to diverse perspectives.
Open to students from all years, the seminar series brings together experienced professionals with our students to explore different aspects of the medical profession, share inspiring experiences, and contribute to students’ career planning. The Career Development Days are also integrated into the Social Transcript initiative, which has been designed to document and support students’ academic and professional development throughout their education.
All sessions are supported by an AI-based simultaneous translation system.

pages.title: The UNESCO Turkey Bioethics Monitoring Group Organized a ‘Neurotechnology Ethics Conference’ | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: UNESCO Turkey National Commission Bioethics Monitoring Group welcomed Assoc. Prof. Dr. Roberto, as an invited speaker, by a webinar on "The Ethics of Neurotechnology (2025): Perspectives from the UNESCO Declaration" on December 22, 2025. Dr. Andorno, a member of the Ad Hoc Expert Group on the Ethics of Neurotechnology (AHEG), contributed to Draft Recommendation together with 23 scholars from different countries and disciplines. The UNESCO Recommendation of Ethics of Neurotechnology was adopted by Member States at the UNESCO General Conference as the first global standard in the cutting-edge technology in November 2025.Prof. Andorno has elaborated that neurotechnologies can interact in two different ways with brain. They can be used to read the brain data (ex. brain scans); and to write on the brain to alter some data or “improve” some brain functions (ex. brain stimulation). Therefore, neurotechnologies provide new diagnostic, preventive and treatment tools for neurological conditions, such as Brain Computer Interface (BCI) or functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI). All these technologies imply that a large amount of neural data can be potentially accessed and misused by third parties (other individuals, employers, the State and so on...). UNESCO Recommendation has emphasized the significance of using neurotechologies based on the values of Respect for human dignity, human rights, and fundamental freedoms; Human health and well-being (priority); Peace and fairness in society; Beneficence, Non-Maleficence, Proportionality, Autonomy and Freedom of Thought, Protection of Neural Data (Inf. Consent) and Mental Privacy, Personal Identity, Non-Discrimination, Gender Equality, Accountability, Trustworthiness, Transparency, Best Interest of the Child.Prof. Andorno concluded that Neurotechnologies have great potential to improve the well-being of neurological patients by providing new diagnostic, preventive, and therapeutic tools. However, these same technologies open the door to unprecedented threats to the core of human personhood, to self- determination, freedom of thought, and mental privacy. The UNESCO Recommendation on the ethics of neurotechnology represents a valuable step by the international community towards ensuring a responsible use of these new technological powers. It is now up to the states to adopt specific legislative measures at the domestic level to implement the values and principles set out in the Recommendation. The webinar ended with Q&As by the audience, international colleagues from diverse disciplines, the members of the Bioethics Group, Profs Hakan Orer, Uygar Tazebay, Ozge Yucel and Yesim Isil Ulman. (UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Neurotechnology, November 2025 https://www.unesco.org/en/ethics-neurotech/recommendation)

UNESCO Turkey National Commission Bioethics Monitoring Group welcomed Assoc. Prof. Dr. Roberto, as an invited speaker, by a webinar on "The Ethics of Neurotechnology (2025): Perspectives from the UNESCO Declaration" on December 22, 2025. Dr. Andorno, a member of the Ad Hoc Expert Group on the Ethics of Neurotechnology (AHEG), contributed to Draft Recommendation together with 23 scholars from different countries and disciplines. The UNESCO Recommendation of Ethics of Neurotechnology was adopted by Member States at the UNESCO General Conference as the first global standard in the cutting-edge technology in November 2025.
Prof. Andorno has elaborated that neurotechnologies can interact in two different ways with brain. They can be used to read the brain data (ex. brain scans); and to write on the brain to alter some data or “improve” some brain functions (ex. brain stimulation). Therefore, neurotechnologies provide new diagnostic, preventive and treatment tools for neurological conditions, such as Brain Computer Interface (BCI) or functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI). All these technologies imply that a large amount of neural data can be potentially accessed and misused by third parties (other individuals, employers, the State and so on...).
UNESCO Recommendation has emphasized the significance of using neurotechologies based on the values of Respect for human dignity, human rights, and fundamental freedoms; Human health and well-being (priority); Peace and fairness in society; Beneficence, Non-Maleficence, Proportionality, Autonomy and Freedom of Thought, Protection of Neural Data (Inf. Consent) and Mental Privacy, Personal Identity, Non-Discrimination, Gender Equality, Accountability, Trustworthiness, Transparency, Best Interest of the Child.
Prof. Andorno concluded that Neurotechnologies have great potential to improve the well-being of neurological patients by providing new diagnostic, preventive, and therapeutic tools. However, these same technologies open the door to unprecedented threats to the core of human personhood, to self- determination, freedom of thought, and mental privacy. The UNESCO Recommendation on the ethics of neurotechnology represents a valuable step by the international community towards ensuring a responsible use of these new technological powers. It is now up to the states to adopt specific legislative measures at the domestic level to implement the values and principles set out in the Recommendation. The webinar ended with Q&As by the audience, international colleagues from diverse disciplines, the members of the Bioethics Group, Profs Hakan Orer, Uygar Tazebay, Ozge Yucel and Yesim Isil Ulman. (UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Neurotechnology, November 2025
https://www.unesco.org/en/ethics-neurotech/recommendation

Message of Dean
Mission & Vision
Mission
Our mission is to train pharmacists, who have high theoretical and practical competentence in pharmacology profession, who have vast knowledge in profession’s expanding areas, who are universally respectful to human rights and the environment, who are sensitive about safe drug production and consumption, who believe in supremacy of science and also who are open to life long learning.
Vision
Our vision is to become internationally-known, leading Pharmacology Faculty in Turkey, by means of having considerable number of projects and publications, qualified and experienced academicians, as wells as centers where we conduct researches for products that are preferred in every field of pharmacology, by utilizing clinical and pharmaceutical industry oriented education models.
Core Values
As Acibadem University Pharmacology Faculty, our core values and principles are as follows:
Allegiance to Ataturks Principles and Reforms
Academic Excellency
Trustworthiness
Commitment to Ethical Values
Innovativeness
Team Mentality
Universality
Human-oriented Mentality
Mentality of Protecting and Defending Student Rights on Every Matter
Dean's Message
The mission of Acıbadem University Faculty of Pharmacy is to educate well-trained, creative, research-oriented, and innovative pharmacists at an international level, ensuring that these pharmacists become highly sought-after professionals employed in every field of pharmacy—primarily in the pharmaceutical industry and hospitals—both in our country and worldwide. Students are provided with access to the state-of-the-art laboratories of Acıbadem University Faculty of Pharmacy, as well as the hospitals and polyclinics within the Acıbadem Healthcare Group, located not only in Istanbul but also in various regions of Turkey.
With the devoted contributions of an experienced academic staff who closely follow scientific developments, the faculty employs interactive, innovative, and motivating educational models in emerging areas of pharmacy. This approach ensures that graduates are fully equipped to work in all areas of the profession (academia, pharmaceutical industry, hospitals, community pharmacies). Throughout the entire education process, the Faculty aims to maintain close collaboration with the pharmaceutical industry, contribute to the advancement of the national pharmaceutical sector by utilizing all available opportunities, conduct R&D studies for the development of herbal, dermatological, and cosmetic products, and design scientific projects with potential for commercialization beyond academia—sharing all these processes together with its students.

pages.title: Analytical Chemistry | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.text:
Home
Undergraduate Programs
Home
Undergraduate Programs
Analytical Chemistry
Basic Pharmaceutical Sciences
Analytical Chemistry
Biochemistry
Pharmaceutical Basic Sciences
History of Pharmacology
Pharmaceutical Microbiology
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
The main purpose of Analytical Chemistry Department is to make sure that the students have enough theoretical and methodological knowledge to do chemical and intrumental analysis of organic and inorganic materials, including drugs. Education focuses on explanation of chemical and intrumental methods’ theoretical aspects and problem solving. One of our goals is to educate individuals who have basic knowledge of analytical chemistry and medicine analysis, who have analytical thinking ability, who are able to become internationally qualified scientists and who also can create industrially applicable ideas.
Research Areas:
Spectroscopic Methods, analytical method validations, separation methods, pharmaceutical quality control
Sor
Cevaplayalım

pages.title: Pharmacognosis | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.text:
Home
Undergraduate Programs
Home
Undergraduate Programs
Pharmacognosis
Pharmaceutical Profession Sciences
Pharmacognosis
Pharmaceutical Chemistry
Pharmaceutical Botanics
Pharmacology
Pharmacy Management
Department of Clinical Pharmacy
Department of Toxicology
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
When we review pharmacology history, we see that it is integrated with pharmacognosis. In addition to being one of the core disciplines in pharmacology education, it involves analysis of naturally-resourced medicine raw materials and also chemical, pharmacological and cultural study of these biologically-oriented substances, which are used in treatment and prevention of diseases. Besides being related to scientific or traditional treatment methods, such as phytotherapy, aromatherapy, homoeopathy, pharmacognosis also pertains to substances and products that are used in folkloric medicine.
Areas of Study:
Naturally-resourced medicine raw materials, active ingredient isolation and structure determination, biological activity and activity lead isolation studies, analytical pharmacognosis (quality control operations), morphological and anatomical analysis of drugs, tissue culture studies.
Sor
Cevaplayalım

pages.title: Pharmaceutical Technology | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.text:
Home
Undergraduate Programs
Home
Undergraduate Programs
Pharmaceutical Technology
Pharmaceutical Technology
Pharmaceutical Biotechnology
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
Pharmaceutical technology is a science field that conducts scientific and technological studies which are required for the designing of various pharmaceutical drug configurations and for their industrial productions and tests, by using natural, synthetic and semi-synthetic active and supplementary ingredients.
The mission of the department is to provide theoretical and practical training and education in pharmaceutical field, for all pharmacology students, to conduct scientific researches in this field and to train experts, scientists and academicians.
The vision of the department is to train pharmacists and scientists, who would be in demand for their knowledge and skillset in every field of pharmacology, especially in pharmaceutical industry and hospitals, by way of the training and education courses provided in English, also with our laboratories which are fully-equipped with current technology and scientific infrastructure and research opportunities.
Areas of Study:
Pharmaceutical technology, formulation of all types of drug configurations, analysis and selection of supplementary ingredients used in the formulas, their interactions with the active ingredients, development of new drug delivery systems, testing the specifications, stabilities and bioavailability characteristics of the developed formulas, development and testing modified drug delivery systems.
Some of the areas of study in Pharmaceutical Technology Department are as follows:
Tablets, Capsules
Particular Systems
Pallets, Microspheres
Transdermal Therapeutic Systems
Controlled Delivery Systems
Parenteral Dosage Types and Parenteral Drug Production
Disperse Systems Formulation (Emulsion, Suspension) in vitro/in vivo analysis
Topically Applied Semi-solid Formulations (Ointment, Gel, Cream) in vitro/in vivo analysis
Adsorption Mechanism Studies
Stability Studies
Studies on Drug Production and Testing Methods
Development of Costemic Product Formulas
Presentation of Consultancy Services on Production Support and Tests
Sor
Cevaplayalım

pages.title: Support Provided to Prof. Günay Yetik Anacak’s Project Under TÜBİTAK 2237-A | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: Our faculty member from the Department of Pharmacology, Faculty of Pharmacy, Prof. Günay Yetik Anacak, is the coordinator of the project titled “Health Sciences Academy: Training on Writing Scientific Research Project Proposals and Career Development for Undergraduate Students in Health Sciences,” which has been accepted within the scope of TÜBİTAK 2237-A – Scientific Education Activities Support.We congratulate our faculty member and wish her continued success.

We congratulate our faculty member and wish her continued success.

About

Academic
Student
Information Package
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
About
This department is committed to training dietitians who are guided by science in the preservation, development and sustainability of health. Our graduates will be able to combine applied education and academics with theory in order to maintain the standards required for a healthy lifestyle. Our graduating dietitians will promote, sustain and renew healthy eating habits based on scientific studies on an individual, social and global level; will be guided by professional ethical regulations; will have a firm belief in team work and will be a qualified healthcare professional embracing preventive and curative practices.

About
The Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation of the Faculty of Health Sciences at Acıbadem University offers a 4-year undergraduate program which includes basic and clinical science lessons as well as practical and theoretical training revolving around the planning and application of treatment programs. The course is taught in Turkish. The aim is to develop the vision of students by supporting education with an intensive English education and monitoring of international literature.

About
In recent years, many changes have taken place in the Turkish healthcare sector with the aim of preserving and improving the health status of society. These changes have led to an increase in competition in the healthcare sector which has continued to grow and evolve. As a result, there is an increased need for managers who can develop more efficient, more productive and more innovative solutions to problems experienced in the field and who possess conceptual, analytical and technical knowledge, skills and talents which can help guide the sector.

pages.title: The 8th Orthopedic Rehabilitation Symposium Was Held at Our University | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: The VIII Orthopedic Rehabilitation Symposium, organized in collaboration with the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation of Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University Faculty of Health Sciences and the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation of Istanbul University–Cerrahpaşa Faculty of Health Sciences, was held on March 22, 2025.The theme of this year’s symposium, which is part of the traditionally organized annual orthopedic symposium series, was “Rehabilitation After Knee Surgery.” Hosted by our university, the symposium brought together many participants interested in the field of orthopedic rehabilitation, including academics, healthcare professionals, and students.During the symposium, post–knee surgery rehabilitation processes, current approaches, and developments in clinical practice were discussed.

The VIII Orthopedic Rehabilitation Symposium, organized in collaboration with the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation of Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University Faculty of Health Sciences and the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation of Istanbul University–Cerrahpaşa Faculty of Health Sciences, was held on March 22, 2025.
The theme of this year’s symposium, which is part of the traditionally organized annual orthopedic symposium series, was “Rehabilitation After Knee Surgery.” Hosted by our university, the symposium brought together many participants interested in the field of orthopedic rehabilitation, including academics, healthcare professionals, and students.
During the symposium, post–knee surgery rehabilitation processes, current approaches, and developments in clinical practice were discussed.

pages.title: “Best Oral Presentation” Award to Acıbadem University | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: At the II Congress on Education, Research and Innovation in Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation, organized by the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation at İzmir Kâtip Çelebi University between April 17–19, 2025, Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University achieved a significant success.The study titled “Early Effects of Simulation-Based Blood Pressure and Electrocardiography Training in Cardiac Rehabilitation”, conducted under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Sema Savcı, faculty member of the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation at the Faculty of Health Sciences, and prepared by research assistants Dilek Çağrı Arslan, Meryem Bektaş Karakuş, Özge Haklı, Yağmur Küçük, and Hatice Kübra Gün, was awarded the First Prize for Best Oral Presentation at the congress.

pages.title: The Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation Held a Welcome Tea Event | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.description: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Nuray Alaca graduated from the Istanbul University School of Physical Therapy and Rehabilitation in 1995. She completed her Master’s degree in the Department of Orthopedic Physiotherapy at the Marmara University Institute of Health Sciences in 2002 with a thesis on different rehabilitation methods for knee prosthesis. She later pursued her doctoral studies in the Department of Physiology at the same institute and received her PhD in 2015 with a dissertation examining the effects of aerobic exercise frequency and the “weekend warrior” exercise model on diabetes parameters and muscle tissue in experimentally induced type 2 diabetic rats.She was awarded the title of Associate Professor in Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation in 2021. Between 2000 and 2011, she worked as a physiotherapy specialist at Marmara University Hospital, primarily with patients in orthopedic and stroke rehabilitation. Since 2011, she has been working as an academic staff member at Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University Faculty of Health Sciences. She has also held several administrative roles, including Head of the Department, Faculty Board Member, and Bologna Coordinator since 2015.Assoc. Prof. Dr. Alaca has 15 articles published in journals indexed in SCI, SSCI, and AHCI, as well as 10 articles in other journals. She teaches undergraduate courses such as Physiology, Exercise Physiology, Manual Therapy, Orthopedic Rehabilitation, Stroke Rehabilitation, and Elastic Taping at Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University.The event played an important role in strengthening social interaction among students and was completed in a friendly and enjoyable atmosphere.

News
News Archive
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour

30/10/2024
Last update date:
13.03.2026
Assoc. Prof. Dr. Nuray Alaca graduated from the Istanbul University School of Physical Therapy and Rehabilitation in 1995. She completed her Master’s degree in the Department of Orthopedic Physiotherapy at the Marmara University Institute of Health Sciences in 2002 with a thesis on different rehabilitation methods for knee prosthesis. She later pursued her doctoral studies in the Department of Physiology at the same institute and received her PhD in 2015 with a dissertation examining the effects of aerobic exercise frequency and the “weekend warrior” exercise model on diabetes parameters and muscle tissue in experimentally induced type 2 diabetic rats.
She was awarded the title of Associate Professor in Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation in 2021. Between 2000 and 2011, she worked as a physiotherapy specialist at Marmara University Hospital, primarily with patients in orthopedic and stroke rehabilitation. Since 2011, she has been working as an academic staff member at Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University Faculty of Health Sciences. She has also held several administrative roles, including Head of the Department, Faculty Board Member, and Bologna Coordinator since 2015.
Assoc. Prof. Dr. Alaca has 15 articles published in journals indexed in SCI, SSCI, and AHCI, as well as 10 articles in other journals. She teaches undergraduate courses such as Physiology, Exercise Physiology, Manual Therapy, Orthopedic Rehabilitation, Stroke Rehabilitation, and Elastic Taping at Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University.
The event played an important role in strengthening social interaction among students and was completed in a friendly and enjoyable atmosphere.

Dear Young Fellows,
We live in a time characterized by the rapid generation and swift dissemination of knowledge. Knowledge being the key power in today's world; knowledgeable individuals represent the most valuable assets of communities. We, therefore, aim at educating and graduating young assets for the community, who have thoroughly conceived the needs of the present and the future, are competent in access to the knowledge they need, have internalized the methodology of scientific thinking and study, are capable of objectively evaluating the knowledge they access and making informed decisions on the basis thereof, respect the human – the environment – the Earth, adhere to democracy and ethic values, and committed to the creation of authentic products rather than imitation.​
The Faculty of Arts and Sciences of Acibadem University welcomes you, the professionals of the future, with the aforementioned mission in the academic year of 2013 – 2014.
The Faculty has commenced its higher education activities, admitting undergraduate students to the Department of Molecular Biology – Genetics and the Department of Psychology. Being always committed to the best, we expect to see driven and excellence – oriented students amongst us on our path toward the accomplishment of our stated mission.
I wish you a bright and successful future.

About
Established in the 2020–2021 academic year, the Acıbadem University Department of Psychology (English) is a dynamic and young department that aims to equip its students with the highest level of professional and academic competence. Having graduated its first cohort in the Spring semester of the 2024–2025 academic year, the department has a strong academic staff who are experts in their fields and who teach courses in line with ethical and scientific principles.
Within our English curriculum, a wide range of elective courses is offered, allowing students to explore various subfields of psychology. In this way, students have the opportunity to discover areas of interest and shape their careers accordingly.
With its fully English-based program, the Department of Psychology (English) has become a department that provides education aligned with current and universal scientific standards, making it suitable for international students as well. With its continuously developing academic staff and infrastructure, our department aims to be among the reputable and leading psychology departments in our country.

Message From Head of Department

About
The Department of Sociology of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences at Acıbadem University opened in the 2016-2017 academic year. The course program is compatible with the sociology courses offered at other universities in Turkey and in Western countries. Much importance is placed on this compatibility in order for students, particularly post-graduate students, not to encounter any obstacles in their education. The program is taught in English which is advantageous for students who wish to participate in student exchange possibilities. Additionally, it helps students benefit from world literature and to undertake their post-graduate studies abroad should they wish to do so.
Sociology is a member of the social sciences family. In order for students to truly grasp the concept of sociology, they need to be familiar with the relation between sociology and social sciences, the common and different ground, and the opportunities for collaboration. The department offers compulsory and elective lessons in all the fields of social sciences in order to meet this requirement.

pages.title: About Faculty | Acıbadem Üniversitesi
pages.text:
Home
Academic
Home
Academic
About Faculty
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
Dean - Prof. Dr. Ata Akın
Engineering is fundamentally focused on developing technologies that improve the quality of life. A good engineer should have a strong send of both life and its quality, as well as a good grasp of the basics of technology and its development processes. Moreover, an engineer must be eager to produce new and innovative technologies. The aim of the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences is on one hand to graduate health technologists and engineers who aim to develop innovative technologies that will increase the quality of health services, backed up with strong basic scientific knowledge, and on the other hand graduate basic science experts who can use these technologies and even contribute to their development. In this context, the mission of our faculty is to educate our students to be critical thinkers, to possess research passion, and who will be competitive in national and international fields while proposing innovative solutions to the problems of the society they live in.
We have established the four most basic departments necessary to accomplish this mission. Students who choose the Department of Molecular Biology and Genetics receive most up to date knowledge on this field, while under Department of Biomedical Engineering they learn the operational principles of technologies used in healthcare. Students of the Department of Computer Engineering learn the basics of this field with a strong emphasis on the concepts and applications data engineering employed in healthcare sector. Basic scientific knowledge, which is the starting point of all students, is given by our Department of Basic Sciences. Throughout their education, students receive training from their expert teachers in our laboratories equipped with the latest technologies.
In our faculty, both basic scientific and applied studies are carried out in areas such as molecular biology and genetics, cancer, neuroscience, neurotechnology, biomaterials, tissue engineering, medical devices, medical biotechnology, drug discovery, artificial intelligence engineering. Students are offered courses and research opportunities in these subjects.
This is a path to be cherished by the most passionate since we are all about improving healthcare. We welcome and educate the privileged students in our state-of-art university.
Ata Akın M.D., Prof.
Dean
Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences

About the Department
The establishment of the “Department of Molecular Biology and Genetics” at our university will not only enhance Turkey’s knowledge base and international recognition in this field, but will also contribute to addressing the shortage of highly qualified professionals needed in the country’s healthcare sector. The most significant feature distinguishing this department from its counterparts is that, in addition to fundamental scientific research, it places strong emphasis on clinical applications, while enabling its students to benefit from the facilities of Acıbadem University and Acıbadem Healthcare Group. Thanks to its state-of-the-art laboratories and modern physical infrastructure, it provides opportunities for world-leading research in the field of health, spanning the journey from laboratory to clinical practice.

pages.text:
Home
Vocational School Of Health Services
Home
Vocational School Of Health Services
About
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
Director - Prof. Dr . Olcay Çizmeli
The aim of Acıbadem University Vocational School of Health Services is to train “Health Technicians.” The programs are designed to keep up with global and national advancements in the healthcare field, equipping students with lifelong knowledge, skills, and experience. This ensures the training of highly qualified health technicians who will serve human health as the right hand of physicians.
Our programs, which consist of four semesters, provide students with not only professional theoretical and practical courses but also general culture courses. This approach helps them develop both professional competence and the qualities of responsible and exemplary individuals in society.
At the Vocational School of Health Services, theoretical courses are delivered by the university’s distinguished academic staff, while practical training takes place in state-of-the-art laboratories and the simulation center (CASE) within Kerem Aydınlar Campus. Additionally, students receive hands-on experience in the hospitals of Acıbadem Healthcare Group, one of the leading healthcare institutions in the country.
Sor
Cevaplayalım

pages.description: The 'physiotherapy' program opened within the scope of Acıbadem University Vocational School of Health Services is a 2-year associate degree program prepared for this purpose. According to the specialist physician's diagnosis and treatment program; this program trains highly competent, knowledge-equipped Physiotherapy technicians who will assist in providing the necessary physical therapy and rehabilitation program to individuals injured for any reason under the supervision of a physiotherapist.
pages.text:

Message from Head of Program
Physiotherapy
Message from Head of Program
Academic Staff
Curriculum
Information Package
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
The 'physiotherapy' program opened within the scope of Acıbadem University Vocational School of Health Services is a 2-year associate degree program prepared for this purpose. According to the specialist physician's diagnosis and treatment program; this program trains highly competent, knowledge-equipped Physiotherapy technicians who will assist in providing the necessary physical therapy and rehabilitation program to individuals injured for any reason under the supervision of a physiotherapist. Physiotherapy technicians are healthcare personnel who help patients provide safe, effective and quality health services through physical therapy and exercise applications under the supervision of a specialist physician and physiotherapist.
The need for physical therapy and rehabilitation services increases  after any injury that causes movement disorders to prevent functional disorders from becoming permanent and to increase the person's quality of life. The success of treatments in this field, is dependent that the application is carried out by well-trained auxiliary health personnel, "Physiotherapy Technicians", under the coordination and supervision of a Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation specialist physician.
Program Head

The aim of Opticianry Program is to raise health technicians who are able to choose optic devices, prepare them in the specified standards and ensure the adaptation of the patient, as prescribed by
ophthalmologists for eliminating or alleviating visual disturbances of the patients with refractor defect.
The program has a 2-year education period and the education language is Turkish.
Within the program, students are given practical basic skills training in the Opticianry Laboratory equipped with modern machinery and devices as well as theoretical education. In addition, students are given training in the applications they will encounter in post-graduate work fields and business training.
Students gain experience in practice by performing compulsory summer internships for 25 days at optical stores at the end of the first year. In addition to the practices in Opticianry Laboratory in
the second year of the program, there are hospital practices in the program where students can work individually with ophthalmologists at the Eye Polyclinics of Acıbadem Healthcare Group hospitals.
Ali Rıza Cenk Çelebi Assoc. Prof.
Head

Dear Cooking Program Prospective Students,
Acıbadem University Cooking Program is a 2-year associate degree program. The main purpose of this program is to train chefs who are competent in their profession, curious and questioning, inquisitive, and open to innovations with practice and theory courses.
Gastronomy is a multidisciplinary field. It works as a whole with many fields such as history, geography, sociology, economy and marketing. In this context, knowing the ingredients in a plate; mastering the historical processes of the products, cooking techniques and nutritional values ​​allows a good chef to make a difference in the sector. It is aimed for you to gain a multi-faceted perspective with our experienced academic staff and guests from various fields of the sector that we will host in our school to share their experiences with you for 4 semesters.
Get ready for this intense and enjoyable process, we will be happy to see you among us.
Lecturer Pelin Kosal
Cooking Program Head

pages.description: The aim of Biomedical Equipment Technology Program is to train biomedical technicians who know the test methodologies, make maintenance repairs and follow up to ensure that the diagnostic and treatment functions of the medical devices can be fulfilled properly. The program has a 2-year education period and the education language is Turkish.
pages.text:

Message from Head of Program
Biomedical Equipment Technology
Message from Head of Program
Academic Staff
Curriculum
Information Package
University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
The aim of Biomedical Equipment Technology Program is to train biomedical technicians who know the test methodologies, make maintenance repairs and follow up to ensure that the diagnostic and treatment functions of the medical devices can be fulfilled properly. The program has a 2-year education period and the education language is Turkish.
In the Center of Advanced Simulation and Education, program students are informed about the indications of the use of many medical devices in the clinic, so that students can learn both the practical operation of medical devices and the effects of medical device problems on the patients, with the help of patient simulators.
CASE is intensively benefited in the first year of program. In the second year of the program, theoretical and practical trainings on tests, calibrations and diagnostics of medical devices are carried out in the Medical Device Test and Calibration Laboratory.
Program students gain pre-graduate professional experience with internships in the hospitals of Acıbadem Healthcare Group and other health institutions which are in cooperation with the university.
Emin AKSOY Assist Prof.
Head

pages.description: Computer Programming is a program related to computer technologies, programming, computer use, software development (in local, intranet or Internet environments), hardware, maintenance-repair, installation and management of computer networks.The need for a technician who specializes in the field of information in our country day by day, is able to produce the right solution in the face of problems, is qualified, adapts to teamwork, develops analytical thinking skills, and can work as a software developer and/or technical support personnel is increasing.
pages.text:
Home
Academic

Programs
Home

About
Computer Programming
About
Academic Staff

University Introduction Booklet
Virtual Tour
Computer Programming is a program related to computer technologies, programming, computer use, software development (in local, intranet or Internet environments), hardware, maintenance-repair, installation and management of computer networks.The need for a technician who specializes in the field of information in our country day by day, is able to produce the right solution in the face of problems, is qualified, adapts to teamwork, develops analytical thinking skills, and can work as a software developer and/or technical support personnel is increasing. In this context, the curriculum of this program, which was opened to gain a profession in a much-needed field, includes courses based on computer hardware and software technologies. In addition to compulsory courses, students will also take elective courses such as System Design, Computer Hardware, Computer Networks, Databases, Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Mobile Programming, Internet Programming and Embedded Systems.
With Computer Programming, which is a rapidly developing field in the world and Turkey, it is aimed to train an intermediate workforce with global competence. Graduates will have a wide range of job opportunities in software companies, computer sales and technical support companies, banks, insurance companies, commercial organizations, internet service providers, internet broadcasting companies, radio-television companies, research companies, stock exchanges, transportation, education, logistics companies and public institutions and organizations in the service sector.

Acibadem University, Institute of Health Sciences combines the strengths of University’s health and life sciences themed establishment and well-experienced faculties with Acıbadem Health Group’s high level health care services in Turkey and abroad for pursuing its interdisciplinary graduate programs.
Basic principles and values of Acıbadem University, Institute of Health Sciences are;
Dignity,
Analytical thinking,
Participation,
Transparency,
Academic freedom,
Respect for ethical values,
Creativity,
Innovation.
Institute of Health Sciences was founded in February 10, 2010. Currently, There are 19 MSc. programs and 8 Ph.D. programs within the 15 Departments of the Institute; which include interdisciplinary cooperation with the Faculty of Medicine, Faculty of Health Sciences, Faculty of Pharmacy and Faculty of Science. Students have the opportunity to share common educational, research and social infrastructures and environment located at the Kerem Aydinlar Campus of the University. In addition, students have access Acıbadem Hospital's clinical and diagnostic facilities to practice research if needed.
Uğur Özbek M.D., Prof.
Director
Our mission;
Being a pioneering, innovative, creative and preferred Institute with its educational and research environments provided in the field of health sciences.
Our vision;
Developing programs that brings creative thinking to the forefront by collaborating with different branches of science and produce solutions to the health problems of the society; Providing strong scientific and technological infrastructure to train independent researchers, experts and practitioners who would innovate their field attached to ethical values.

ORPHEUS
ORganisation of PhD in Biomedicine and Health Sciences EUropean System -ORPHEUS is an international organization formed from representatives of higher education institutions, aiming to develop and disseminate basic standards and good practices in doctoral education in Biomedicine and Health Sciences, to safeguard the PhD as a research degree by supporting graduate schools, faculties and departments, research students and their supervisors, through quality assurance mechanisms, advocation of best practices,professional development and training.
As of 29 May 2017, Health Sciences Institute has been a member of ORganisation of PhD in Biomedicine and Health Sciences EUropean System-ORPHEUS program. Institute of Health Sciences completed the evaluations in compliance with the recommendations of “ORPHEUS/AMSE Good Practices for Doctoral Education” and received the ORPHEUS Evaluation Certificate on October 23, 2021.
Click here
to access the ORPHEUS website.
Click here to access the “Good Practices for Doctoral Education” booklet based on ORPHEUS-AMSE-WFME standards.

1. How can I access all information (fields of study, course contents/TYYC information package, academic staff, etc.) of graduate programs?
You can access the areas of study of Graduate programs, their course contents/TYYC information package, academic staff information etc. via (
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu
)
2. When will applications for graduate programs begin?
Graduate programs accepting students, student quotas for these programs, documents required for application, application and exam dates and other conditions are announced on the website of the University and Institute under the announcement section (
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/duyurular?tags=saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu
) within the date ranges specified in the academic calendar each semester.(
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/ogrenci/ogrenci-isleri/akademik-takvim
).
3. What are the student admission requirements for graduate programs?
The application conditions of the graduate programs that will accept students are on the web page of the University and the Institute (
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/duyurular?tags=saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu
) under the announcement section every semester within the date ranges specified in the academic calendar. You can reach the application conditions of the programs via the link of the application conditions (
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/programlar
) on the web page of the relevant program.
4. Which Master’s Degrees are available at your institute?
The List of graduate programs in our institute is available via
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/programlar
.

6. I earned the right to enroll in graduate programs. How can I find out the registration dates?
Application, foreign language exam, interview exams, and registration dates of the graduate programs are specified in the graduate academic calendar of our University and announced on the website of the University and the Institute under the announcements section within the date ranges specified in the academic calendar each semester (
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/duyurular?tags=saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu
)
7. Do I have to come to the campus for the application and registration procedures for graduate programs? Can I send my application/registration documents by e-mail/mail?
Applications to graduate programs are made electronically, and it is obligatory for the student to make the registration in person. For detailed information about the application and registration process, you can contact the Student’s Affairs Office via
ogrenci.isleri@acibadem.edu.tr
.
8. Is ALES score required for applications to graduate programs?
It is not required to have taken ALES for applying to Master’s Degrees without thesis.
For the Master’s Degree with thesis, it is required to have the ALES score, not less than 55 points, which will be determined by the Senate in the type of score applied.
For Doctoral Programs, it is required to have the ALES score, not less than 55 points, which is determined by the Senate and in the type of score which is submitted for application.
ALES is not required for graduates of doctorate / proficiency in art / specialty in medicine / specialization in dentistry / specialization in veterinary medicine / specialization in pharmacy. The 65 points determined by the Acıbadem Mehmet Ali Aydınlar University Senate are taken into account, regardless of the type of score at the application to the graduate program or the field of specialization.
From the candidates who will apply to doctoral programs in basic medical sciences, it is required for those who are the graduates of medical faculties to have a bachelor's degree and a basic medical score of at least 55 points or more from TUS; or it is necessary to have the ALES score requirement determined by the Senate, provided that it is not less than 55 standard points from the numerical part of ALES.
9. Is a foreign language (YDS/YOKDIL) score required for applications to graduate programs?
YDS/YOKDIL and YDS/YOKDIL equivalent exam score requirements must be met for applications to all graduate programs. Candidates who do not have YDS/YOKDIL and YDS/YOKDIL equivalent exam scores cannot apply to doctoral programs. Candidates applying to graduate programs who do not have YDS/YOKDIL and YDS/YOKDIL equivalent exam scores have to take the foreign language proficiency exam conducted by our University.
10. How and by whom are candidates evaluated for graduate programs?
Evaluation of the candidates in applications to graduate programs is determined by the examination juries of our University following the 20th Article of our Regulation on Postgraduate Education, Training and Examination.
11. How can I apply as a student with special student status?
Pursuant to the Article 31 of the Regulation on Graduate Education and Training of the Council of Higher Education, students enrolled in a graduate program in a higher education institution can be accepted as special students with the approval of the institute department head in order to be registered for graduate courses in other higher education institutions.
In order to apply to our graduate programs as a special student, you are required to submit the decision of the institute board of directors of your higher education institution that you are registered to, showing that it is appropriate for you to continue your education as a special student at our university (the course contents of our university's programs can be found on the web pages of the relevant academic units), your student certificate, your transcript, your identity card photocopy, your resume and your application petition to the Institute Directorate.
12. When should I deposit the tuition fee?
Tuition fees are paid at the beginning of each semester at the course registration/registration renewal phase. Students who do not pay their tuition fees cannot make registration or renew their registration, except for scholarship students.
13. How should I register my course? Can someone else register on behalf of me?
Course registrations are made online each semester through the Student Information System (https://obs.acibadem.edu.tr/oibs/ogrenci/login.aspx ) at the date ranges specified in the academic calendar. No other person can make the course registration on behalf of the student.
14. I am having problems with course registration/add course screens. What should I do?
By explaining the problem you are experiencing, you should send the screenshot/image of the problem you are experiencing on the course registration / add-drop screens to
ogrenci.isleri@acibadem.edu.tr
For detailed information about your course registration/add-drop process, you should contact the Student’s Affairs Office.
15. How can I find detailed information about graduate education and training processes?
You can reach detailed information about graduate education and training processes via
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
16. I am in the graduation stage. How can I get detailed information on this process?
You can reach detailed information on graduate graduation processes via
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
17. I want to deregister. What should I do?
The student who wants to cancel the registration is supposed to go to the Student’s Affairs Office in person and fill out the dismissal form. Then s/he applies to the institute directorate with a dismissal form and a petition explaining the reason for deregistration. The student's request for deregistration is decided by the Institute Administrative Board and notified to the Rectorate.
18. Is there any obligation to attend classes?
It is obligatory to attend classes, laboratory and application studies, and other studies deemed appropriate by the instructors. A student's absence during the semester cannot exceed 30% of the total time for each course. A student who cannot meet the required rate for attendance from a course is considered unsuccessful due to absenteeism from that course.
19. How is the grade of success determined in a course?
The achievement of the students in a course is determined by the course success grade. The course success grade is obtained by evaluating the student's success in studies such as midterm exams, applied studies, homework, and the final exam together.
20. Can I take another course instead of the elective course I failed?
Instead of the failed elective course, another elective course with the same credit and ECTS value can be taken. After the relevant course is selected through the Student Information System (OBS), an application is made to the Institute Directorate with a petition. In the petition, the situation is explained by specifying the previously unsuccessful course code and name and the newly chosen course code and name.
21. What are the graduation requirements in graduate programs?
You can reach detailed information on graduate graduation requirements via
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
22. How can I appeal my exam result?
The student who objects to an announced exam result or grade makes this objection within five working days following the announcement of the result by a written petition to the Head of the relevant Department. This objection is forwarded to the course supervisor within two working days. If the lecturer sees an error in the evaluation, he/she makes the necessary correction and reports the result to the Head of the Department within two working days at the latest. The decision to be made by the lecturer as a result of the examination and approved by the Head of the Department is final. The corrections made in the success grade are notified to the Institute Directorate in writing by the Head of the relevant Department. The grade change is decided by the Institute Administrative Board and notified to the Rectorate.
23. In which cases are students dismissed from the Institute?
Students are dismissed from the Institute in the following cases: a) The student makes a written request to the institute directorate that he/she wants to dismiss him/her voluntarily.
b) Expulsion of the student from the University.
c) Students who cannot successfully complete the credit courses and seminar courses in the curriculum at the end of four semesters in the Master’s Degree with thesis and three semesters in the Master’s Degree without thesis, or who cannot fulfill the success conditions/criteria stipulated by the University within this period.
d) Students who failed in their thesis study or did not enter the thesis defense in the Master’s Degree with thesis.
e) Students who have been accepted to the doctoral program with a master's degree with thesis, who have not successfully completed the required credit courses in a maximum of four semesters or who cannot achieve the minimum grade point average required by the University.
f) Students who have been accepted to the doctoral program with a bachelor's degree but have not successfully completed the required credit courses in a maximum of six semesters or cannot achieve the minimum grade point average required by the University.
g) Students who successfully complete their credit courses in doctoral programs, who are successful in the proficiency exam and whose thesis proposal is accepted, cannot complete their thesis by the end of twelve semesters for those who are accepted with a master's degree, or by the end of fourteen semesters for those who are accepted with a bachelor's degree.
h) Students whose thesis proposal is rejected twice in the doctoral program.
i) Students whose thesis proposal is accepted in the doctoral program and who are found unsuccessful two times in a row or three times intermittently by the thesis monitoring committee.
24. Can I switch between programs?
Transfer between thesis and non-thesis Master’s Degrees is made by student application and EYK decision upon the recommendation of the Institute Department, within the date range specified in the academic calendar, in case a quota is opened for the relevant program. The student cannot apply for more than one transfer between the thesis and non-thesis Master’s Degrees. The student whose transfer application is approved is responsible for completing the requirements of the new program.
25. What is the minimum (normal) and maximum education period of the Master’s Degrees with thesis?
The duration of the Master’s Degree with thesis, excluding the time spent in scientific preparation, and starting from the semester in which the courses related to the program are given, regardless of whether they are registered for each semester or not is a
minimum of four semesters
and a
maximum of six semesters
.
26. In which case is the student dismissed from the university in a Master’s Degree with thesis?
If a student has not successfully completed his/her credit courses and seminar course included in the curriculum at the end of the four semesters, or has not met the success conditions/criteria stipulated by the University during this period, or has not completed his/her thesis or entered the thesis defense within the maximum period given to him/her, he/she will be dismissed from our university.
27. When should the thesis advisor appointment be made in the Master’s Degree with thesis?
In the Master’s Degree with thesis, one of the faculty members with the qualifications to be determined by the Senate for each student by the Head of the Department is assigned as a thesis advisor by
the end of the first semester at the latest
. For detailed information on the appointment of a thesis advisor, you can check
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
28. How long do I have to submit my thesis proposal in the Master’s Degree with thesis?
The thesis topic of the student and the title of the thesis to be proposed are determined together with the student advisor, taking into account the student's tendencies. After the thesis topic has been evaluated by the relevant Department Head, it is forwarded to the Institute Directorate and finalized with the approval of the EYK. For detailed information about the thesis proposal process, you can check
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
29. In the Master’s Degree with thesis, a second advisor must be appointed for my thesis. Could the second advisor be from outside the university?
The second thesis advisor to be appointed in cases where the nature of the thesis work requires more than one thesis advisor, may be appointed from within or out of the University staff with at least a doctorate degree. For detailed information on the appointment of a thesis advisor, you can check the link.
30. I came to the stage of defending my master's thesis. What should I do?
You can check the link for detailed information about the thesis defense
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
31. What is the minimum (normal) and maximum study period of the non-thesis Master’s Degree?
The period to complete the Master’s Degree without Thesis, excluding the study period spent in scientific preparation, starting from the semester in which the courses related to the program are given, regardless of whether the students are registered for each semester or not is
minimum of two semesters
and
maximum of three semesters
. At the end of this period, the student who fails or is not able to complete the program is dismissed from the University.
32. When is the latest when my project advisor is assigned in the non-thesis Master’s Degree?
In the non-thesis Master’s Degree, one of the faculty members with the qualifications to be determined by the Senate is assigned for each student by the Head of the Department among the staff of the University by the end of the first semester at the latest. For detailed information you can check the
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
33. What is the minimum (normal) and maximum study period of doctoral programs?
The normal duration of a Ph.D. program is
eight academic semesters
, excluding the period spent in the Scientific Preparation Program, for those admitted with a
master's degree with thesis
regardless of if they are registered for each semester, starting from the semester in which the courses related to the program they are enrolled in are given. The maximum duration of the Ph.D. program is
twelve academic semesters
. In Doctoral Programs admitting students with a Bachelor’s degree, the normal duration is
ten academic semesters
, and the maximum duration is
fourteen academic semesters
.
34. By what time should my Doctoral thesis advisor be appointed at the latest?
At the Institute of Health Sciences, it is mandatory to appoint a thesis advisor no later than the end of the first semester. For detailed information on the appointment of a thesis advisor, you can visit
this link
.
35. What should I do to take the Doctoral qualification exam?
For detailed information on the application process for the doctoral qualification exam, you can check
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
.
At the end of the third semester; Completing 90 ECTS and seminar courses, having an academic average of at least 3.00/4.00, and successfully completing all credit courses at the end of the 4th semester at the latest, approved by the Institute Orpheus Commission. A student who has at least one thesis topic/field, prepared according to a national or international refereed journal, a research article or review or case report submission letter in which the candidate is one of the first two names, or made an oral or poster presentation summary at a scientific meeting, can apply the doctoral qualification exam.
36. When is the thesis monitoring committee formed?
For the student who is successful in the doctoral qualification exam, a thesis monitoring committee is formed within one month at the latest, approved by the doctoral qualification committee, recommended by the Head of the Department, and finalized by the decision of the EYK. For detailed information about the thesis monitoring committee formation process, you can check
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
37. When is the thesis proposal prepared? How to defend a thesis proposal?
The student who successfully completes the doctoral qualification exam must orally defend the thesis proficiency study proposal, which includes the purpose, method, and study plan of the research to be carried out, in front of the thesis monitoring committee within six months at the latest from the qualification exam date. The student distributes the thesis proposal to the members of the thesis monitoring committee at least fifteen days before the oral defense. The thesis monitoring committee examines the thesis proposal submitted by the student and takes the candidate to the defense exam. The exam is open to the audience. For detailed information about the doctoral thesis proposal process, you can check
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
38. When does the thesis monitoring committee meet? How often should the Thesis Monitoring Committee (TİK) reports be prepared?
The thesis monitoring committee for the student whose thesis proposal is accepted gathers
twice a year
as once in January-June and once in July-December. Thesis monitoring committee meetings accompanied by
Doctoral Thesis Interim Report and Doctoral Thesis Monitoring Activity Report are conveyed with a cover letter on EBYS
. (For Turkish forms
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/enstitu-formlar-ve-dilekceler
, for English forms
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
). Forms with original signatures are submitted to the Institute simultaneously. You may check
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/surec-akis-semalari
for detailed information on the process of the thesis monitoring committee reports.
39. I got a medical report. What do I need to do to get it processed? Is the time I have a report included in my education period?
A student who receives a rest report from university hospitals or health institutions with official patient admission authority due to an excuse related to health submits the report given or approved by the University's Health, Culture and Sports Department Health Center to the relevant Department Head
within three working days from the end of the reporting period
. The student is considered excused within the periods specified in the report and does not take midterm exams. The grades or results of the midterm exams taken during the reporting period are deemed invalid. After the end of the report, the student can take the make-up exams.
Report periods are not taken into account in the calculation of education periods. Students who have a report of five weeks or more in a semester can be considered on leave for that semester
.
40. How can I freeze my registration?
Students apply to the Head of the Department with a petition explaining the reasons for freezing their registration and the relevant documents in its annex. Applications deemed appropriate by the Head of the Department are forwarded to the Institute Directorate for evaluation. Registration freezing decisions can be taken for the situations specified in the 59th Article of our University's Regulation on Postgraduate Education, Training and Examination The situation of the students is evaluated and decided by the Institute Administrative Board and reported to the Rectorate.
41. How do official documents (forms, petitions) reach the Institute?
Students submit the relevant documents to the Department Chairs, and the Department Chairs forward them to the Institute with an official cover letter (EBYS).
42. When must the thesis proposal be submitted in Thesis-Based Master's Programs at the latest?
It must be submitted no later than the end of the second semester. Students who have not determined their thesis topic cannot enroll in the Thesis Study course in the third semester.
43. Which documents must be submitted to the Institute before the Thesis Defense Examination?
Master's Thesis Originality Report (Form 6) (1 copy)
Master's Thesis Submission and Thesis Defense Jury Recommendation Form (Form 8) (1 copy)
One spiral-bound copy of the thesis
44. When and how should the Thesis Defense Jury members be appointed?
The Thesis Defense Jury members must be appointed no later than the end of the 6th semester, with the process starting by the end of the 4th semester. The jury consists of three members: one thesis advisor, one external member from another university, and one internal member from the university/program. If there is a second thesis advisor, the jury must have five members.
The Thesis Defense Jury is proposed by the Department Chair through an official cover letter via the EBYS system, along with the Master's Thesis Submission and Thesis Defense Jury Recommendation Form, the Master's Thesis Originality Report, and one spiral-bound copy of the thesis.
45. Which documents must be submitted to the Institute after the Thesis Defense Examination and within what time frame?
Thesis Monitoring and Evaluation Form (Form 10) (3 copies for each jury member)
Master's Thesis Defense Minutes (Form 7) (3 copies)
Master's Thesis Approval Form (Form 9) (8 copies)
These documents must be submitted to the Institute no later than 3 working days after the Thesis Defense Examination.
46. What is the total duration for students who receive corrections during the Thesis Defense Examination?
For doctoral students: No later than 6 months
For thesis-based master's students: No later than 3 months
47. What is the deadline for submitting the bound thesis to the Institute after the Thesis Defense Examination date?
After the Thesis Defense Examination, the student completes the necessary revisions and submits the final version of the thesis in spiral-bound form to the Institute. The thesis will be reviewed by the Institute official according to the Thesis Writing Guide. The bound version of the thesis, approved by the Institute official, must be submitted to the Institute no later than 1 month after the Thesis Defense Examination.
48. What other documents must a student bring along with the bound thesis when reaching the binding stage of the thesis process?
A student who has received approval from the Institute for binding their thesis must submit the following documents to the Institute:
2 bound copies of the thesis
2 CDs containing the thesis in PDF format
The KVKK Confidentiality Declaration Form
2 Thesis Data Entry Forms
49. What is the document submission process for thesis studies requiring Ethics Committee Approval?
For thesis studies requiring Ethics Committee approval, students must submit the Thesis Proposal Form along with the Ethics Committee approval to the Department Chair.
50. When should the thesis advisor be appointed in Thesis-Based Master's Programs?
The thesis advisor must be appointed by the end of the first semester.
51. When should the thesis advisor be appointed for Doctoral Programs?
The thesis advisor must be appointed by the end of the second semester.
52. What is the submission process for the Student-Advisor Agreement Form for doctoral students?
The Student-Advisor Agreement Form must be submitted to the Department Chair along with the Thesis Advisor Proposal Form for forwarding to the Institute.
53. What happens if doctoral students do not submit their Thesis Monitoring Committee (TİK) reports on time?
If a doctoral student fails to submit their Thesis Monitoring Committee (TİK) reports twice consecutively or three times intermittently (in case of non-submission), their relationship with the university will be terminated.
54. Can a student's second thesis advisor be a member of the Thesis Monitoring Committee (TİK) in a Doctoral Program?
According to Article 54, paragraph 2 of our university's Graduate Education-Teaching and Examination Regulations, the second thesis advisor cannot be a member of the Thesis Monitoring Committee (TİK).
55. What is the difference between course advisor and thesis advisor appointments?
At the beginning of the student's first registration period, the Department Chair informs the Institute about the course advisor appointment for course registration. The thesis advisor appointment, however, must be made by the end of the first semester.
56. Where can we access the forms of the Institute of Health Sciences?
You can access the forms here:
https://www.acibadem.edu.tr/akademik/lisansustu/saglik-bilimleri-enstitusu/enstitu-formlar-ve-dilekceler
57. Can a word that is an abbreviation be used in a thesis title?
Commonly accepted abbreviations such as TÜBİTAK and DNA may be used in thesis titles.
